issue: The only push/PR-triggered workflow is docs.yml (doc-link check + a soft warning). The android-build workflows are workflow_dispatch / opt-in-by-commit-message only. No workflow runs backend tsc, eslint, or jest, and none runs frontend tsc/jest. The 465 backend + 120 frontend passing tests never gate a PR or push — a regression cannot fail CI.
fix: Add a CI job (on push/PR) running `cd backend && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test` and `cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit && npx jest --ci`.
note: docs.yml runs on push(master)+PR but only does node scripts/check-doc-links.mjs + a soft ::warning docs-drift nudge; the three android-build workflows are workflow_dispatch or opt-in via commit-message contains([auto-build]/[ab]/[auto]). grep for tsc/jest/test/lint across all workflows finds none. No workflow gates PR/push on tests or typecheck.

issue: `npm run lint` (eslint) exits 1 with 1042 errors + 269 warnings in src alone (486 no-unsafe-member-access, 272 no-unsafe-argument, 157 no-unsafe-assignment, 63 no-unused-vars, ...); only 155 are auto-fixable by --fix, so the gate cannot pass. Confirmed instance: backend/src/water-quality/water-quality.service.ts:4 imports unused `ForbiddenException`.
fix: Fix the unsafe-any/unused-var violations (add types to Supabase query results, remove dead imports) or relax the type-aware rules in eslint.config.mjs, until `eslint "{src,apps,libs,test}/**/*.ts"` exits 0.
note: Ran `npx eslint src/**/*.ts`: exit 1, 1311 problems (1042 errors, 269 warnings), only 155 --fix-able — matches finding exactly. eslint.config.mjs enables recommendedTypeChecked (unsafe-* + no-unused-vars as errors). water-quality.service.ts:4 imports ForbiddenException, sole occurrence is the import line — genuinely unused. `npm run lint` uses --fix but still exits 1.

issue: createAutoAlert types severity as 'info'|'warning'|'critical' and the entity comment (alert.entity.ts:41) documents info/warning/critical, but AlertCenterService.emit (alert-center.service.ts:57 via create) persists engine severities of 'watch'. The column is free text so nothing crashes, but two write paths store different vocabularies for the same field; any consumer filtering on 'warning' will miss 'watch' rows.
fix: Normalize on one severity vocabulary (map 'watch'→'warning' on emit, or widen the type) across both write paths.
note: createAutoAlert types severity as info|warning|critical; AlertCenterService.emit (alert-center.service.ts:57) calls the same AlertsService.create into the same free-text severity column with AlertSeverity=info|watch|critical, and engine-alert.service.ts writes 'watch'. Both vocabularies land in one column; a filter on 'warning' misses 'watch' rows. LOW appropriate (free text, no crash).

issue: RolesGuard and PermissionsGuard read user.roles, but the global JwtAuthGuard sets req.user = { id, email } with no roles and neither guard/@Roles/@Permissions is referenced anywhere; if ever applied they would silently fail-closed (return false) on every request. GetUserId decorator (auth.decorators.ts:18) likewise reads request.user.userId which is always undefined.
fix: Delete RolesGuard/PermissionsGuard/roles.enum/permissions.enum/GetUserId, or populate user.roles in JwtAuthGuard before using them.
note: JwtAuthGuard sets req.user={id,email} (no roles). RolesGuard/PermissionsGuard read user.roles and fail-closed. GetUserId returns request.user.userId which is always undefined (guard sets id). Grep confirms RolesGuard, PermissionsGuard, and GetUserId have zero references outside their own definitions/specs — dead code + latent bug. LOW appropriate.

issue: POST /login-otp/verify has no @Throttle, so it inherits only the global 60/min bucket while its sibling /login-otp/request is capped at 5/min (SENSITIVE_THROTTLE). The verify endpoint is the actual brute-force surface for the emailed OTP and is 12x more permissive than the send endpoint.
fix: Add @Throttle(SENSITIVE_THROTTLE) to verifyLoginOtp().
note: login-otp/verify (l.211) has @Public + @HttpCode but no @Throttle, while sibling login-otp/request (l.203) has @Throttle(SENSITIVE_THROTTLE)=5/60s. Global bucket is 60/60s (app.module.ts:63-64), so verify (the OTP brute-force surface) is 12x more permissive. MEDIUM appropriate.

issue: POST /oauth/google (l.342) and POST /oauth/truecaller (l.361) are @Public account-creation/login surfaces with no @Throttle, inheriting only the 60/min global bucket while password signup/signin are capped tighter (SENSITIVE/AUTH_THROTTLE). Credential/token-guessing and account-spray on these paths is under-limited relative to the other auth routes.
fix: Add @Throttle(AUTH_THROTTLE) to the oauth/google and oauth/truecaller handlers.
note: oauth/google (~l.342) and oauth/truecaller (~l.361) are @Public with no @Throttle, inheriting only the 60/60s global bucket, whereas signup=@Throttle(SENSITIVE 5/60s) and signin=@Throttle(AUTH 10/60s). OAuth login/creation surfaces under-limited relative to peers. MEDIUM appropriate.

issue: POST /resend-verification is @Public with no @Throttle and takes an untyped inline body ({ email: string }) instead of a validated DTO, so the whitelist ValidationPipe cannot strip/validate it. It is an unthrottled email-sending (bombing) surface bounded only by the global 60/min bucket and accepts arbitrary payloads.
fix: Add @Throttle(SENSITIVE_THROTTLE) and replace the inline body with a class-validated ResendVerificationDto (IsEmail).
note: resend-verification (l.570) is @Public with no @Throttle and takes inline `@Body() body: { email: string }` (not a DTO, so whitelist ValidationPipe can't validate it). Unthrottled email-send surface bounded only by global 60/60s. MEDIUM appropriate.

issue: POST /credit binds @Body() to Partial<CreditLedger>, which erases to the native Object type at runtime, so Nest's global ValidationPipe skips it entirely. The purpose-built CreateCreditDto (which its own docstring says omits `id` specifically to stop hijack) is never used. Service does repo.create({...body}) then repo.save; a client-supplied `id` makes save() UPDATE an existing row, and because userId is spread after `...data` the row's userId is reassigned to the attacker. POST {id: <victim ledger id>, dealerName, principal, startDate} overwrites and takes over another user's credit ledger row; negative principal/repaid also pass unvalidated.
fix: Change the param to `@Body() body: CreateCreditDto` so validation runs and `id`/`userId` cannot be client-supplied.
note: @Body() Partial<CreditLedger> erases to Object so ValidationPipe is skipped; CreateCreditDto (which omits id by design) is unused. service.create does repo.create({...data, userId, repaid}) then save(); client-supplied id makes save() UPDATE an existing PK row and userId (spread after ...data) is forced to the attacker, taking over another user's ledger row. Real cross-tenant mass-assignment. CRITICAL correct.

issue: recordRepayment does `row.repaid = round2(Number(row.repaid) + amount)` with no sign/bound check, and the controller types the body as the plain interface RepaymentBody (erases to Object, so ValidationPipe never runs). A negative `amount` decreases repaid and inflates the outstanding balance; over-repayment pushes repaid above principal silently. Financial ledger corruption from an unvalidated number.
fix: Reject amount <= 0 (throw BadRequestException) in recordRepayment, or bind to a validated DTO with @IsNumber()@Min(0.01).
note: recordRepayment has no sign/type check and RepaymentBody is a bare interface (no validation); negative/NaN/string amount corrupts the ledger. But ownership is enforced (row.userId !== userId -> Forbidden at credit.service.ts:87), so it only corrupts the caller's OWN row. Self-scoped data-integrity gap, not cross-tenant — HIGH overstated.

area: crops harvest unvalidated body / mass assignment
issue: harvest(@Body() harvestData: {...}) uses an inline type (no DTO class) so the global ValidationPipe skips it; service then spreads {...harvestData, status} into cropsRepository.update, accepting negative/NaN harvestWeightKg and letting arbitrary crop columns (status, stockingCount, pondId) be overwritten.
fix: Introduce a HarvestCropDto with @IsNumber @Min(0) harvestWeightKg + @IsDateString, and only assign the two whitelisted fields.
note: harvest(@Body() inline-typed object) bypasses ValidationPipe; crops.service.ts:197 spreads {...harvestData, status:'harvested'} into cropsRepository.update, so any entity column in the body (pondId, stockingCount, actualHarvestDate) is written unwhitelisted and harvestWeightKg is unbounded. status is forced, but pondId reassignment is a real cross-tenant/mass-assignment concern. HIGH defensible.

issue: crops.harvest() sets status='harvested' while closeCycle() (also called by harvests.create full) sets status='completed', and the entity documents only 'active|completed|cancelled'; the two code paths leave the same real event in two different states, breaking status-based filtering.
fix: Pick one terminal status (e.g. 'completed') and use it in both harvest() and closeCycle().
note: crops.service.ts:199 harvest() sets status='harvested'; closeCycle() (line 221, also reached via harvests.service full-harvest -> closeCycle) sets 'completed'; entity comment (crop.entity.ts:142) documents only active|completed|cancelled. Two paths for the same event yield divergent, undocumented states.

issue: disease_library is a global, non-tenant-scoped table, yet createDisease (l.29), seedDiseases (l.45), updateLibrary (Put library/:id), and removeLibrary (Delete library/:id) carry only the global JwtAuthGuard with no RolesGuard/@Roles. Any authenticated farmer can create, overwrite, seed, or delete the shared disease reference data seen by every tenant.
fix: Add @UseGuards(RolesGuard) + @Roles(Role.Admin) to the four library-mutation handlers.
note: disease library POST(29)/seed(45)/PUT(55)/DELETE(60) carry no @UseGuards; only the global JwtAuthGuard applies. Any authenticated user can create/seed/overwrite/delete the shared, non-tenant-scoped disease_library. No RolesGuard/@Roles present.

area: authorization / shared reference data mutable by any user
issue: Global disease-library POST (line 29), PUT (55), DELETE (60) and reference species/hatchery/broodstock create/update/delete (reference.controller.ts:25 onward) and news create/update/delete (news.controller.ts:20) have no role/admin guard. RolesGuard exists (auth/guards/roles.guard.ts) but @Roles is never used anywhere in the codebase, so every authenticated farmer can rewrite or wipe shared catalog/content data.
fix: Wire an admin RolesGuard (@UseGuards(RolesGuard) + @Roles('admin')) onto all write/delete endpoints for disease library, reference catalogs, and news.
note: Confirmed superset of 12: reference.controller.ts create/update/delete for species/hatcheries/broodstocks (lines 25-101) and news.controller.ts create/update/delete (20-42) have no role guard. Verified @Roles is used in zero files and RolesGuard is only defined, never applied. Every authenticated user can rewrite/wipe shared catalog/content.

issue: GET /disease/record/crop/:cropId → disease.service.ts:237 findRecordsByCrop(cropId) queries by cropId with no user parameter and no ownership check, so any authenticated user can read any crop's disease occurrence history by guessing/enumerating cropIds.
fix: Add @UseGuards(OwnershipGuard)+@OwnsResource('Crop','cropId','pond.farm.userId','READ') to the route.
note: GET record/crop/:cropId (disease.controller.ts:74) has no OwnershipGuard; findRecordsByCrop queries by cropId only. Sibling controllers guard this exact path with @OwnsResource('Crop','cropId','pond.farm.userId','READ'). Global JwtAuthGuard means any authenticated user can read another farm's records with a known cropId (UUIDs, so enumeration is hard but cross-tenant access is real). HIGH is correct.

issue: GET disease/record/crop/:cropId has no OwnershipGuard/@OwnsResource; findRecordsByCrop returns every record for the given cropId with no ownership check, so any authenticated user reads any other farm's disease records by guessing/enumerating cropIds. Sibling data controllers (chemical/plankton/microbiology/treatments) all guard this exact path with @OwnsResource('Crop','cropId','pond.farm.userId','READ').
fix: Add @UseGuards(OwnershipGuard) + @OwnsResource('Crop','cropId','pond.farm.userId','READ') to findRecordsByCrop.
note: Same defect as id 3, confirmed. Read-only cross-tenant leak (cropIds are UUIDs, not trivially enumerable) — HIGH is more accurate than CRITICAL for a read-only IDOR.

issue: PATCH /disease/record/:id → disease.service.ts:245 updateRecord(id,dto,userId) fetches the record by id and updates it with no ownership verification (only stamps updatedById), so any authenticated user can modify any farm's disease record, including re-pointing its cropId/severity.
fix: Add @UseGuards(OwnershipGuard)+@OwnsResource('DiseaseRecord','id','crop.pond.farm.userId','WRITE_MANAGEMENT') and assert access inside updateRecord.
note: PATCH record/:id (disease.controller.ts:79) has no ownership guard; updateRecord (service:245) fetches by id and updates with no ownership check, only stamps updatedById. Any authenticated user can modify any farm's record. Siblings guard with @OwnsResource('...Data','id','crop.pond.farm.userId').

issue: PATCH disease/record/:id has no OwnershipGuard and updateRecord() (disease.service.ts:245) updates by id with no ownership check; any authenticated user can overwrite any farm's disease record.
fix: Add @UseGuards(OwnershipGuard) + @OwnsResource('DiseaseRecord','id','crop.pond.farm.userId') to updateRecord.
note: Same cross-tenant write defect as id 2, confirmed at disease.controller.ts:79 / service:245. Cross-tenant tamper of records justifies CRITICAL.

issue: DELETE /disease/record/:id has only the global JwtAuthGuard and no ownership check; disease.service.ts:261 removeRecord(id) looks up the record by id and deletes it with zero user/crop scoping, so any authenticated user can delete any farm's disease records.
fix: Add @UseGuards(OwnershipGuard)+@OwnsResource('DiseaseRecord','id','crop.pond.farm.userId','WRITE_MANAGEMENT') on the route and verify ownership in removeRecord like microbiology does.
note: DELETE record/:id (disease.controller.ts:88) has only global JwtAuthGuard, no OwnershipGuard; removeRecord (service:261) deletes by id with no user/crop scoping and never receives userId. Any authenticated user can delete any farm's records. Microbiology guards the equivalent route.

issue: DELETE disease/record/:id has no OwnershipGuard and removeRecord() (disease.service.ts:261) does no ownership check and does not even receive the caller's userId; any authenticated user can delete any farm's disease record by id.
fix: Add @UseGuards(OwnershipGuard) + @OwnsResource('DiseaseRecord','id','crop.pond.farm.userId') to removeRecord.
note: Same cross-tenant delete/data-loss defect as id 1, confirmed at disease.controller.ts:88 / service:261.

issue: sendEmail logs Brevo 4xx/5xx and returns void with no throw, retry, or queue; callers cannot tell delivery failed. profiles.controller inviteFriend (line 66) returns {success:true} even when Brevo rejected the send, so users see success for mail that never left.
fix: Return a boolean/throw from sendEmail on !res.ok and have callers surface the failure; add a bounded retry for 5xx.
note: sendEmail (email.service.ts:37-78) returns void, logs Brevo !res.ok/catch errors and swallows them; inviteFriend (profiles.controller.ts:66-67) awaits it then unconditionally returns {success:true}. Real silent-failure. Note: only sendInviteEmail is a live caller (OTP/verify/reset go through supabaseAuthService, not this service), so practical blast radius is invite emails.

issue: Recipient email addresses are logged at info level ('Email sent via Brevo API to ${to}: ${subject}') and again in the error branch (l.76), writing user PII into application logs on every transactional email (OTP, verification, password reset).
fix: Drop or hash the ${to} address in these log lines, logging only the subject/outcome.
note: Lines 73 and 70 (and warn at 44/76) log full recipient ${to} at log/error level. Real PII-in-logs. Scope caveat: live path is invite email; OTP/verify/reset methods here are dead code, so those addresses don't actually reach these lines today.

issue: sendVerificationEmail/sendPasswordResetEmail/sendOtpEmail/sendWelcomeEmail/sendPasswordChangedNotification are never called (active auth uses supabase.auth for these mails); only sendInviteEmail is live, so most of the Brevo integration is untested cruft.
fix: Remove the unused email methods or route the real auth flows through EmailService instead of supabase.auth.
note: grep confirms sendVerificationEmail/sendPasswordResetEmail/sendOtpEmail/sendWelcomeEmail/sendPasswordChangedNotification have no callers; auth uses supabaseAuthService's own same-named methods. Only sendInviteEmail is live. Dead cruft confirmed.

issue: No @UseGuards/@OwnsResource on any route and FeedProduct has no farmId/userId column, so any authenticated farmer can POST/PATCH/DELETE the globally-shared feed-product catalog, mutating or wiping reference data used by every tenant.
fix: Add an admin-only role guard (or OwnsResource) to create/update/remove; leave findAll public-read.
note: Global APP_GUARD is JwtAuthGuard (authentication only). feed-products.controller has no role/owner guard on POST/PATCH/DELETE and FeedProduct entity has no farmId/userId column — it's a global catalog. Any authenticated user can mutate/delete shared reference data for all tenants. HIGH is apt.

area: feed-records fasting guard bypass on update
issue: The fasting-day enforcement (quantityKg must be 0 when isFasting) runs only in create(); update() spreads updateDto directly, so a PATCH setting isFasting with a nonzero quantity is not blocked (and isFasting, not a column, is silently dropped).
fix: Apply the same fasting/inventory reconciliation logic in update() or reject isFasting on PATCH.
note: update() (l.141-152) does repository.update(id, {...updateDto}); the create()-only fasting guard (l.48-56) is not applied, and isFasting is not a mapped column so TypeORM drops it while quantityKg (a column) is written — a fasting PATCH with nonzero quantity is unchecked. LOW is apt.

issue: create() deducts inventory stock via adjustStock, but remove() deletes the feed record without restoring stock and update() never reconciles a changed quantityKg, so inventory permanently drifts on edits/deletes.
fix: On remove re-add the previously deducted quantity; on update apply the delta to inventory.
note: create() deducts via adjustStock (l.66-72); remove() (l.154-158) deletes the record with no re-add; update() (l.141-152) never applies a quantity delta to inventory. Permanent drift on edit/delete confirmed.

issue: Inventory is decremented before the feed record is saved with no surrounding transaction, so if the record insert fails the stock is left phantom-deducted with no matching record.
fix: Wrap adjustStock + record save in a single dataSource.transaction.
note: adjustStock (l.67-71) runs before recordsRepository.save (l.91) with no dataSource.transaction wrapping them; a save failure leaves stock phantom-deducted with no matching record. Confirmed.

issue: trayNumber is @IsNumber with no @Min/@IsInt, so zero, negative, or fractional tray numbers are accepted into an int column.
fix: Use @IsInt @Min(1).
note: trayNumber @IsNumber only (dto:28-29); entity col is int (entity:37). Accepts 0/negative/fractional.

issue: remainingFeedStatus is validated only as @IsString despite the entity documenting a fixed set ('empty'|'few_left'|'a_lot_left'), so arbitrary strings persist and any downstream feed-adjust logic branching on these values silently mis-advises.
fix: Replace @IsString with @IsIn(['empty','few_left','a_lot_left']).
note: @IsString only, arbitrary strings persist; but sole consumer pond-context.service.ts:295-302 guards to valid-or-null, so 'mis-advises' is overstated — validation gap real, downstream impact minimal.

issue: findAll returns every tray check across all accessible farms with no take/skip; the same unbounded pattern exists in sampling.service.ts:62 and harvests.service.ts:34, so long-running farms return huge unpaginated payloads.
fix: Add PageOptionsDto take/skip (as water-quality/feed-records already do) to these list queries.
note: feeding-tray-checks:48, sampling:62, harvests:34 all getMany() with no take/skip; only scoped by accessible farmIds.

issue: getCycleFinancials sums Number(amount) in JS floating point and returns totalRevenue/totalExpenses/netProfit/marginPercent unrounded; reports.getFinancialReport re-sums those floats unrounded. Values like 12345.67 can surface as 12345.670000000002 in API responses.
fix: Apply round2() to the returned totals (revenue, expenses, profit) as pnl.service.ts already does.
note: getCycleFinancials returns unrounded floats (106-112); reports.service.ts:172-173 re-sums unrounded; pnl.service uses round2 as the fix precedent.

issue: create() only authorizes createDto.pondId (assertCanAccessPond) but then trusts createDto.cropId verbatim without checking crop.pondId === createDto.pondId. A user with access to their own pond can POST an expense with their pondId but a victim's cropId; the row lands under the victim's crop. PnlService/getCycleFinancials sum expenses by cropId, so the attacker's bogus cost pollutes another tenant's P&L, break-even and margin.
fix: Load the crop and assert crop.pondId === createDto.pondId (and caller access to it) before saving when cropId is supplied.
note: create() checks only pondId (assertCanAccessPond); persists createDto.cropId verbatim at line 53 with no crop.pondId===pondId check. getCycleFinancials/findByCycle group by cropId → cross-tenant P&L pollution. Real IDOR.

issue: complete(@Body() payload:{...}) inline type bypasses validation, so actualWeightKg/actualPricePerKg can be negative or NaN and are multiplied into actualRevenue and written to a Transaction.
fix: Add a CompletePlanDto with @IsNumber @Min(0) on weight and price and @IsDateString on date.
note: Inline @Body() payload type (controller:54-59) is erased at runtime; ValidationPipe cannot validate native Object metatype, so negative/NaN weight*price reach actualRevenue and a Transaction row.

issue: getCycleSummary (route pond/:pondId/summary) sums revenue/expense across the entire farm and all time via t.farmId only, ignoring pondId and the cycle entirely, so the per-pond 'cycle summary' reports farm-wide lifetime totals.
fix: Scope the transaction sums to the pond/crop (join through pond or filter by cropId), not just farmId.
note: getCycleSummary sums by t.farmId only (112,119), ignoring pondId/cycle; route pond/:pondId/summary returns pondId → farm-wide totals mislabeled per-pond. Note: Transaction entity has no pondId/cropId, so suggested cropId-filter fix needs schema change.

issue: completePlan inserts a new 'income' Transaction on every call with no status/idempotency guard, so a double-tap or offline retry of PATCH :id/complete double-books harvest revenue into the farm P&L.
fix: Return early (or throw) if plan.status==='completed' before writing the Transaction, or upsert the transaction keyed by plan id.
note: completePlan (harvest-plans.service.ts:83) always saves an income Transaction with no plan.status==='completed' guard; sibling modules in this repo implement insert-or-return idempotency, this financial write does not.

issue: optimize(@Body() body: OptimizeBody) uses an interface (no DTO class), so abwNow/adgNow/nNow/feedPricePerKg are never validated; negative or NaN values produce negative/garbage net-profit and optimal-day advice with no error.
fix: Convert OptimizeBody to a class with @IsNumber @Min(0) on the numeric fields.
note: OptimizeBody is a TS interface (line 21) so ValidationPipe cannot validate it; numeric fields taken via ?? 0 with no @Min(0), negatives/NaN reach the projection math and can be persisted.

issue: create() has no client-minted id/idempotency guard (unlike feed/water/sampling/mortality), so an offline-queue retry double-inserts the harvest and calls closeCycle twice, double-counting biomass and revenue.
fix: Add optional @IsUUID id to CreateHarvestDto and short-circuit-return an existing row within the same cropId.
note: CreateHarvestDto lacks optional @IsUUID id and create() (line 19) plain-inserts + calls closeCycle; feed/water/sampling/mortality all have the documented insert-or-return idempotency this one is missing.

issue: Health check pings only Postgres; it never reports RedisService.useMemory. When Redis is down the app silently degrades to a per-instance token store (documented as BROKEN when scaled), yet /health still reports 'ok' — ops have no signal the multi-instance auth store is broken.
fix: Inject RedisService, expose a status flag, and include checks['redis'] = { status: useMemory ? 'degraded' : 'up' }.
note: RedisService.useMemory exists (redis.service.ts:11) and gates the token store, but health.controller.ts probes only Postgres; no redis/degraded signal exposed.

area: health check / broken-instance stays in rotation
issue: check() returns HTTP 200 even when the 'SELECT 1' database probe fails (only body.status flips to 'error'). Render/load-balancer probes that key on HTTP status treat the instance as healthy and keep routing traffic to a DB-disconnected instance.
fix: Throw ServiceUnavailableException (503) when checks.database.status === 'down' so the probe fails on HTTP status.
note: check() returns plain 200 object with only body.status flipping to 'error' on DB-down; root render.yaml healthCheckPath=/api/health keys on this so probe never fails on status code.

issue: check() returns a plain object, so Nest always responds HTTP 200 even when the DB probe fails (body {status:'error'} but status code 200). Render/most orchestrators treat 2xx as healthy, so a DB-down instance keeps receiving traffic — the readiness probe can never fail.
fix: When database.status==='down', throw new ServiceUnavailableException(payload) so the endpoint returns 503.
note: Same defect as 49 (200 on DB-down); severity lowered to MEDIUM — Render uses health path mainly at deploy time, a separate /api/liveness exists, so 'keeps routing traffic' is overstated.

issue: No helmet / security-response-headers middleware anywhere; bootstrap enables CORS + cookie-parser but never sets HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, or a CSP (helmet is not even a dependency in backend/package.json). API responses ship with default Express headers, leaving the app open to clickjacking, MIME-sniffing, and downgrade attacks.
fix: npm i helmet and add app.use(helmet()) before app.enableCors() in main.ts.
note: No helmet in backend/package.json (grep empty) and no security-header middleware in main.ts; lowered to MEDIUM since this is a JSON-only API behind Render TLS — clickjacking/MIME-sniffing largely N/A, HSTS/no-sniff still worthwhile.

issue: No app.enableShutdownHooks() and no SIGTERM handler. On deploy Render sends SIGTERM; Node terminates immediately without draining in-flight HTTP requests, so writes in progress are lost and OnModuleDestroy cleanup never runs.
fix: Call app.enableShutdownHooks() and handle SIGTERM/SIGINT to await app.close() before exit.
note: main.ts:16-60 has no enableShutdownHooks() and bootstrap only registers .catch->process.exit; grep confirms zero SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers repo-wide. Render's SIGTERM kills Node without draining.

issue: No process-level 'unhandledRejection'/'uncaughtException' handler anywhere in the backend; under Node's default a single stray un-awaited promise rejection (e.g. a future fire-and-forget push/email) terminates the entire process, taking down all in-flight requests.
fix: Register process.on('unhandledRejection'/'uncaughtException') handlers that log and, for rejections, avoid hard-exit; wire to app.close() on fatal.
note: Grep confirms no unhandledRejection/uncaughtException handlers anywhere in src/. Modern Node crashes the process on an unhandled rejection; the 'future fire-and-forget' framing is speculative but the gap is real today.

area: measurement / DOC derived from unowned crop
issue: create() verifies ownership of dto.pondId but deriveDoc looks up dto.cropId with no check that the crop belongs to that owned pond; a caller can pass another tenant's cropId and have its stockingDate used to compute the stored DOC (minor incorrectness / info inference).
fix: Validate that dto.cropId's pond matches dto.pondId (and is owned) before deriving DOC.
note: create() asserts dto.pondId ownership (line 49) but deriveDoc (242) does findOne({id:cropId}) with no check crop.pondId==dto.pondId or is owned; another tenant's stockingDate drives the stored DOC, leaking it via inference.

issue: computeCropPnl authorizes via cropsService.findOne (crop owner only) while expenses.getCycleFinancials authorizes via farmAccess VIEW_FINANCIALS (owner or manager). A farm manager can see per-cycle financials but is denied the P&L rollup of the same crop — inconsistent authorization on the same financial data.
fix: Gate computeCropPnl through farmAccess VIEW_FINANCIALS on the crop's pond, matching the expenses path.
note: computeCropPnl->cropsService.findOne is owner-only (crops.service.ts:118 via pondsService.findOne owner check); expenses.getCycleFinancials uses assertCanAccessPond VIEW_FINANCIALS (owner+manager). Manager sees cycle financials but 403 on PnL — inconsistent on the app's own capability model.

issue: Alkalinity's confidence age uses chemistryAsOf, which is derived solely from the latest ammonia record's timestamp (line 138/151), not alkalinity's own measurement time — so alkalinity freshness is wrong whenever ammonia and alkalinity were measured on different dates. Likewise pH/Temperature/Salinity (lines 327,333,341) use records[0].recordedAt (newest entry) even when their non-null value was carried forward from an older record, scoring stale carried-forward values as fresh. Both inflate the confidence score the engines attach to their advice.
fix: Track each parameter's own source-record timestamp in resolveWaterQuality and use it for that parameter's ageDays.
note: chemistryAsOf=amm.at (line 151, ammonia's ts) but Alkalinity ageDays uses chemistryAsOf (356) — wrong when measured on different dates. pH/Temp/Salinity (328/335/342) use wq.recordedAt=records[0].recordedAt (newest) while resolveWaterQuality carries forward older non-null values, scoring stale carried-forward values as fresh. Both inflate confidence.

issue: pond.entity.ts marks geometry_type, construction_type, depth_m and calculated_area_m2 as nullable:true, but migration 1771597711215-InitialSchema.ts:30-41 sets all four NOT NULL with no DB default. With synchronize:!isProduction (app.module.ts:81) dev boots and ALTERs these columns to DROP NOT NULL every start, so the dev schema silently diverges from prod; any insert path that omits one (seed/bulk import/future endpoint) passes in dev but raises not-null-violation only in production.
fix: Remove nullable:true on those four columns in pond.entity.ts so the entity matches the migration (or add DB defaults in a migration).
note: pond.entity.ts marks geometry_type/construction_type/depth_m/calculated_area_m2 nullable:true (71-113); migration 30-41 SETs all four NOT NULL, no default. synchronize:!isProduction (app.module.ts:81) means dev ALTERs to DROP NOT NULL each boot -> dev/prod schema divergence; an insert omitting one passes dev, fails prod.

issue: Engine/finance features (feed-advisor, disease-warning, harvest-timing, simulations, measurement, pnl, pond-context) authorize via the owner-only pondsService.findOne/cropsService.findOne (throws Forbidden unless pond.farm.userId===userId), while the OwnershipGuard-based controllers grant managers/workers access; a farm manager can log water quality but is 403'd from generating a feed plan or viewing P&L on the same pond.
fix: Route these services through farmAccess.assertCanAccessPond/findOneAccessible with the appropriate capability instead of the owner-only findOne.
note: feed-advisor/disease-warning/harvest-timing/pond-context/pnl/measurement all authorize via owner-only pondsService.findOne or cropsService.findOne (grep-confirmed), while OwnershipGuard+@OwnsResource grants managers/workers. Members are locked out of engine/finance features. Note: crops.findOne is documented 'STRICT... feeds economics' so economics strictness is partly deliberate, but it still contradicts VIEW_FINANCIALS (owner+manager) — genuine inconsistency.

issue: remove() hard-deletes a pond checking only activeCycleId; onDelete:CASCADE then destroys ALL historical crops, feed, water-quality, harvests, mortality and sampling for that pond, contradicting the doc comment 'Only allowed for ponds with no cycles ever'.
fix: Block delete when any crop has ever existed for the pond (count crops>0 -> ConflictException); otherwise require archive.
note: remove() at ponds.service.ts:360 only checks activeCycleId, reachable via DELETE /ponds/:id (controller:95); migrations show crops/water_quality/sampling/feed_records/harvest_plans/expenses/alerts FK to ponds ON DELETE CASCADE, so historical (closed-cycle) data is destroyed despite the 'no cycles ever' doc.

issue: The products marketplace catalog (price, salePrice, stock) has only the global JwtAuthGuard (authentication) and NO authorization. create, update, updateStock and remove have no role/admin check, so any authenticated farmer or worker can create products, rewrite prices, change stock, or delete the entire catalog. `UseGuards` is imported but never applied.
fix: Add an admin RolesGuard (@UseGuards(RolesGuard) @Roles('admin')) to the write endpoints of ProductsController.
note: Only global JwtAuthGuard (app.module.ts:180); RolesGuard exists but is applied in zero controllers; write endpoints have no role check, any authenticated user can create/update/updateStock/delete the shared catalog.

issue: updateStock uses @Body('quantity') quantity: number with no DTO/validation and sets stock to the absolute value. A negative quantity drives stock below zero, and a non-integer/string value reaches the integer `stock` column and errors at the DB layer.
fix: Bind a validated DTO with @IsInt()@Min(0) for quantity.
note: updateStock binds @Body('quantity') raw primitive (no DTO class) so ValidationPipe is a no-op; service sets stock to absolute value, allowing negative or type-mismatched writes.

issue: Product entity has no owner column (global catalog) yet POST/PATCH/PATCH:id/stock/DELETE on /products carry only the JwtAuthGuard, so any authenticated user can edit pricing/stock or delete products the whole platform sees; same pattern on feed-products, news and reference (species/hatcheries/broodstocks) controllers.
fix: Gate all create/update/delete routes on these global catalogs behind an admin authorization check (a real admin role, since RolesGuard is currently unused).
note: Product entity (product.entity.ts) has no owner column; write routes carry only JwtAuthGuard; RolesGuard verified unused across all controllers. Products claim proven; sibling controllers asserted but not individually re-verified here.

issue: GET /profiles (findAll) has no @Public but any authenticated user reaches it; profiles.service.ts:28 returns profilesRepository.find() — every user's full profile row including email — with no scoping.
fix: Delete this endpoint (or restrict it to return only the caller's own profile / public fields).
note: findAll() returns profilesRepository.find() (full rows incl. email); no @Public but global JwtAuthGuard permits any authenticated user, no owner scoping.

issue: inviteFriend takes an inline { toEmail: string } with no DTO/@IsEmail, so the global ValidationPipe does nothing; unvalidated/garbage recipient addresses are passed straight to Brevo.
fix: Introduce an InviteDto with @IsEmail() toEmail.
note: inviteFriend uses inline { toEmail: string } (no class, no @IsEmail); global ValidationPipe cannot validate a bare TS type; value flows straight to sendInviteEmail/Brevo.

area: Multi-tenant isolation (IDOR read + write via GET)
issue: GET /profiles/:id calls profilesService.upsert(id, user.email) with no id===user.id check: for an existing id it returns another user's full profile (email included), and its upsert branch will overwrite a victim profile's empty email with the CALLER's email, or create a junk profile row stamped with the caller's email for any non-existent id.
fix: Guard with if (id !== user.id) throw ForbiddenException (mirror the PATCH handler), and use a read-only findOne instead of upsert.
note: GET /profiles/:id findOne calls upsert(id, user?.email) with no id===user.id check (unlike PATCH); reads any user's full profile, and upsert's `if(!profile.email && email)` branch overwrites victim's empty email with caller's, or creates junk row for non-existent id.

issue: deleteAccount() deletes the local public.users row (and cascaded farm data) FIRST, then calls Supabase auth.admin.deleteUser in a try/catch that only logs on failure and still returns HTTP 204 'deleted'. If the auth delete fails, the auth.users identity survives; on the user's next login Supabase updates auth.users (last_sign_in_at), firing the on_auth_user_updated mirror trigger (supabase_setup.sql:114) which re-INSERTs a public.users row (ON CONFLICT id) — resurrecting a 'deleted' account that can still sign in, while the user was told deletion succeeded.
fix: Delete the Supabase auth user BEFORE the local rows and let its failure abort the request (throw), so the identity is gone before/with the mirror source.
note: deleteAccount deletes public.users+profiles first (lines 118-121), then Supabase auth in a log-only try/catch (126-132) returning 204. on_auth_user_updated fires handle_new_user on any auth.users UPDATE (e.g. last_sign_in_at on next login), which INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE re-creates public.users (supabase_setup.sql:75-100,114-117). Resurrection + still-signable identity is real.

issue: sendToUser only catches non-2xx from Expo; Expo returns HTTP 200 with body data[].status==='error'/details.error==='DeviceNotRegistered' for stale tokens, which is ignored — the token is never cleared and sendToUser returns true, so the backend keeps pushing to dead tokens forever.
fix: Parse res.data.data[0]; on 'DeviceNotRegistered' call clearToken(userId) and return false.
note: axios throws only on non-2xx; res.data is never read, so Expo's HTTP-200 body with data[].status==='error'/DeviceNotRegistered is ignored and returns true — stale tokens never cleared. Real but impact is only wasted pushes; LOW.

issue: No client.on('error') listener is attached; the service leans on ioredis' internal 'Unhandled error event' handling and only reacts via retryStrategy, so transient mid-session Redis errors are neither logged nor trigger the memory fallback until the retry cap is hit.
fix: Add this.client.on('error', ...) to log and, on terminal failures, invoke enableMemoryFallback.
note: No this.client.on('error', ...) anywhere in the service; only retryStrategy reacts. Accurate improvement.

issue: retryStrategy returns null after 3 failures and flips useMemory=true permanently; once on the per-instance in-memory store the service never attempts to reconnect, so even after Redis recovers 2FA temp tokens and Truecaller nonce replay protection stay per-instance until a full process restart.
fix: Keep the ioredis client reconnecting (drop the times>3 cap or add a periodic reconnect probe) and reset useMemory=false on the 'ready' event.
note: retryStrategy returns null when times>3 (stops reconnecting) and enableMemoryFallback sets useMemory=true; useMemory is never reset and no 'ready'/reconnect path exists, so fallback is permanent until process restart.

issue: getDashboardSummary(userId, farmId) (GET /reports/dashboard?farmId=) never checks that the caller owns/belongs to farmId before counting that farm's active/total ponds, low-stock items and today's feed usage; any authenticated user can read another farm's operational metrics by passing an arbitrary farmId.
fix: At the top of getDashboardSummary add: await this.farmAccess.assertCanAccessFarm(userId, farmId, 'READ') before running the counts.
note: getDashboardSummary (lines 33-75) runs counts scoped only by farmId with no farmAccess.assertCanAccessFarm — unlike sibling getFinancialReport (136) and getCycleAnalysis (79). Controller passes raw ?farmId with no guard. Cross-tenant read of pond/low-stock/feed metrics confirmed.

issue: create() hard-codes cropId: pond.activeCycleId and ignores the DTO's optional cropId, so a sampling record for a specific non-active crop cannot be recorded and lands with null cropId when no active cycle exists.
fix: Use createDto.cropId ?? pond.activeCycleId.
note: CreateSamplingDto.cropId is optional (dto line 22-23) but create() hard-codes cropId: pond.activeCycleId (service line 47), ignoring it; null when no active cycle. Confirmed LOW.

issue: FCR/ADG/survival/feeding/expected-harvest DTOs have @IsNumber with no @Min, so negative feed, negative days, or negative counts are accepted; calculateFcr also returns 0 when harvestWeightKg===0, masking a division error as a valid result.
fix: Add @Min(0) to quantity/count/day fields and return null (or throw) for zero-denominator FCR.
note: calculation.dto.ts: all Fcr/Adg/Survival/Feeding/ExpectedHarvest fields are @IsNumber with no @Min — negatives accepted. calculateFcr returns 0 when harvestWeightKg===0 (service line 35), masking zero-denominator. Confirmed LOW.

issue: biomass reads query params via Number(stockCount)/Number(averageWeightG) with no validation, so a missing or non-numeric query yields NaN in the response body.
fix: Validate with a DTO (ParseFloatPipe / @IsNumber) and reject NaN.
note: @Query params typed number but Nest does not coerce/validate without a pipe; Number(undefined)/Number('abc')=NaN reaches calculateBiomass. Minor imprecision: JSON.stringify(NaN) emits null, so body is {"biomassKg":null} not literal NaN, but the garbage-in/no-validation defect is real.

issue: getRecommendedFeedingRate calls the service with only averageWeightG and never forwards a species param, so the elaborate per-species FR tables (monodon/scampi/indicus) in the service are unreachable via the public endpoint and every caller gets vannamei rates.
fix: Accept @Query('species') and pass it through to getRecommendedFeedingRate.
note: Controller line 126 only forwards averageWeightG; service getRecommendedFeedingRate defaults species='vannamei'. The monodon/scampi tables (service lines 140-153) are unreachable via the only public endpoint. Confirmed dead per-species path.

issue: simulated totalCost = totalFeedCost + baselineOtherCosts, but baselineNetProfit (line 99) is recorded revenue − all expense transactions with no separate feed term. For FeedChange scenarios the projected feed cost is added to the simulated side only (and double-counted if feed is already among the logged expense transactions in baselineOtherCosts), so profitDifference is systematically wrong.
fix: Put baseline and simulated on the same basis: subtract the baseline feed cost from baselineOtherCosts before adding projected totalFeedCost, or model feed as a first-class term on both sides.
note: baselineNetProfit (rev - all expenses incl. logged feed) vs simulatedNetProfit (rev - projectedFeed - all expenses) are not same-basis. In profitDifference baselineOtherCosts cancels, leaving a null FeedChange scenario (growth=0) yielding profitDifference = -totalFeedCost instead of ~0, and reported totalCost double-counts feed if logged as an expense. Systematic error confirmed.

issue: SupabaseService (~280 lines, a parallel auth/user/token data layer) is never injected or registered in any module — fully dead; its cleanupExpiredTokens() has no scheduler/caller so verification_tokens and refresh_tokens would accumulate unbounded if it were ever wired.
fix: Delete SupabaseService (the active path uses TypeORM + supabase.auth); if kept, schedule cleanupExpiredTokens via @nestjs/schedule.
note: grep confirms SupabaseService only appears in its own file — never imported, injected, or in any module providers. No ScheduleModule/@Cron in the repo, so cleanupExpiredTokens has no caller. Fully dead.

issue: Financial write endpoints (POST /transactions, POST /expenses, POST /credit) have no idempotency key. A mobile client retrying a timed-out request double-records income/expense/loan rows, silently corrupting farm financial summaries.
fix: Accept a client idempotency key (e.g. referenceNumber/UUID) with a unique constraint and no-op on duplicate insert.
note: transactions.service.create() does farm-access check then create+save with no dedup; referenceNumber column is nullable and non-unique. Retried timed-out POST double-records. Notably water-quality.service already implements idempotent replay via a client id, so the pattern exists in-repo but transactions lacks it.

issue: recordedAt is a @CreateDateColumn (server insert time) and the create DTO exposes no measurement-timestamp field, so offline records synced later (the app supports idempotent replay) are stamped with the drain time, not when the reading was taken.
fix: Add an optional @IsDateString recordedAt to CreateWaterQualityRecordDto and persist it instead of relying on CreateDateColumn.
note: recordedAt is @CreateDateColumn (insert time); CreateWaterQualityRecordDto exposes id (idempotency key, replay supported at service lines 34-70) but no measurement-timestamp field, and record is built by spreading createDto so recordedAt is not settable. Late offline sync is stamped with sync time. Confirmed.

issue: `npm run test:e2e` fails 6/6 tests. app.e2e-spec.ts uses TypeOrmModule.forRoot({type:'sqlite',...}) but sqlite3 is not installed -> DriverPackageNotInstalledError ('SQLite package has not been found installed'), beforeEach times out at 5000ms. core-flow.e2e-spec.ts:32 imports the real AppModule needing a live DB and fails to connect, tearing down mid-run ('trying to import a file after the Jest environment has been torn down'). The e2e suite cannot run locally at all.
fix: Add sqlite3 to backend devDependencies for app.e2e, and gate core-flow.e2e behind a provisioned test DB (or spin an in-memory Postgres/sqlite for AppModule) so `npm run test:e2e` can pass locally.
note: Suite is non-runnable locally: app.e2e-spec fails (times out at 5004ms — sqlite3 IS in devDependencies/node_modules but its native binding is not built, `require('sqlite3')` => 'Could not locate the bindings file', so the SQLite driver load fails after teardown); core-flow.e2e (6 it() tests) needs live Postgres and cannot connect. Corrections: mechanism is a missing/unbuilt native binding, not a missing devDependency, so the proposed fix 'add sqlite3 to devDependencies' is moot (it's already there — needs rebuild or dropping sqlite); count is 7 tests across 2 suites (1 + 6), not 6/6. Test-infra only, so severity MEDIUM.

issue: APP_FLOW.md says the password-reset flow uses deep link `upcheck://`, but the app's actual scheme is `upcheckapp://` (app.config.ts:9 scheme:'upcheckapp'; App.tsx:7 prefixes:['upcheckapp://'] mapping reset-password → ResetPassword). A tester following the doc opens a non-registered scheme.
fix: Change `upcheck://` to `upcheckapp://` in APP_FLOW.md:28.
note: APP_FLOW.md:28 ResetPassword row says deep link `upcheck://`; app.config.ts:9/35 scheme is `upcheckapp`, App.tsx:7 prefixes ['upcheckapp://']. Wrong scheme.

area: Docs vs code — public endpoint surface
issue: FEATURES.md claims '`GET price*` is `@Public()`', implying both GET /india/price and GET /india/price-feeds are unauthenticated. In india.controller.ts only listFeeds (GET price-feeds, line 58-59) carries @Public(); priceForCount (GET price, line 45) has no @Public() and requires a bearer token.
fix: Change the note to '`GET price-feeds` is `@Public()`' (or add @Public() to GET price if it was meant to be open).
note: FEATURES.md:187 note 'GET price* is @Public()' overclaims; india.controller.ts:45 priceForCount (GET price) has no @Public, only listFeeds line 58-59 does; no class-level @Public, so GET price needs auth.

issue: ACCOUNT_DELETION.md promises account deletion removes 'all farm data you own … (cascade)' and relies on ON DELETE CASCADE from the users row (profiles.service.ts:118 does DELETE FROM users), but credit_ledgers stores user_id as a plain @Column with no ManyToOne relation and no onDelete (credit-ledger.entity.ts:21) — so credit-ledger rows are neither cascade-deleted nor block the delete, leaving orphaned per-user financial records after 'deletion', contradicting the doc.
fix: Add a @ManyToOne(() => User, { onDelete: 'CASCADE' }) relation on credit_ledgers.user_id (and verify every user-owned table has it), or explicitly delete credit_ledgers in deleteAccount().
note: Migration 1780300500000-CreateCreditLedgers.ts explicitly creates credit_ledgers with 'No foreign keys'; entity user_id is plain @Column. deleteAccount() (profiles.service.ts:113) only DELETE FROM users/profiles, so per-user credit rows are orphaned, contradicting the cascade/full-deletion promise.

area: content — Play Store legal docs incomplete
issue: The public-hosting legal docs required for the Play Store listing still contain unfilled placeholders: PRIVACY_POLICY.md:51 '[support@yourdomain.com]' and '[Registered office address]', :3 '[EFFECTIVE DATE]'/'[LAST UPDATED]'; TERMS_AND_CONDITIONS.md:33 '[State], India' and '[support@yourdomain.com]'; ACCOUNT_DELETION.md:19/30 '[support@yourdomain.com]' and '[30] days'. Play Console requires a reachable, complete privacy-policy and data-deletion URL — these documents cannot be published as-is.
fix: Fill every [PLACEHOLDER] (contact email, address, jurisdiction, dates, retention window) in the three docs/legal markdown files and host them at real URLs.
note: Placeholders present: PRIVACY_POLICY.md:3 [EFFECTIVE DATE]/[LAST UPDATED], :51 [support@yourdomain.com]/[Registered office address]; TERMS:33 [State]/[support@yourdomain.com]; ACCOUNT_DELETION:16/19/27/30 [30] days & [support@yourdomain.com]. Not publishable as-is.

issue: Doc instructs "Bump version/versionCode per release in app.config.ts", but app.config.ts has no versionCode field and android/ is committed (bare workflow, per this same doc line 12), so versionCode is governed by frontend/android/app/build.gradle:96 (=1); following the doc changes nothing and the upload keeps versionCode 1.
fix: Rewrite the instruction to bump versionCode in android/app/build.gradle:96 (or enable EAS remote/autoIncrement) and drop the app.config.ts reference.
note: Doc:45 says bump version/versionCode in app.config.ts, but app.config.ts has no versionCode field, eas.json has no autoIncrement, android/ committed (bare), so versionCode is governed by build.gradle:96 (=1); following doc leaves versionCode 1.

issue: The release buildType uses signingConfig signingConfigs.debug, so any locally produced release AAB/APK is signed with the shared debug keystore (password 'android') and is rejected by Play; only EAS-managed builds are safe, making the native project a signing footgun.
fix: Define a real release signingConfig backed by an upload keystore, or document that release builds must go through EAS credentials only.
note: build.gradle:115 release buildType uses signingConfig signingConfigs.debug (debug keystore, password 'android'); local release builds are debug-signed and Play-rejected. Only EAS-managed credentials override signing.

issue: The comment claims truecaller-sdk:2.7.0 is "used by the native bridge in com.upcheck.app.TruecallerAuthModule", but no such class exists (main/java only has MainApplication.kt and MainActivity.kt); the JS path uses the autolinked @dhana-cs/react-native-truecaller module (src/native/TruecallerAuth.ts:17), so the comment misleads maintainers about where Truecaller is wired.
fix: Correct the comment to state the SDK backs the autolinked @dhana-cs TrueCallerModule, not a nonexistent local module.
note: build.gradle:159-160 comment names com.upcheck.app.TruecallerAuthModule as the bridge, but src/main/java has only MainApplication.kt & MainActivity.kt; the string TruecallerAuthModule exists only in this comment. JS uses @dhana-cs/react-native-truecaller (TruecallerAuth.ts:17). Comment is misleading.

issue: RECORD_AUDIO is requested, but the camera is used only for QR scanning (frontend/src/screens/farms/AddWorkerScreen.tsx:11,163) with no audio/video capture anywhere in src/; it is a sensitive permission requiring Data Safety justification and adds review friction. Doc PLAY_STORE_LAUNCH.md:14 flags it, unremediated.
fix: Remove RECORD_AUDIO (add tools:node="remove") or configure expo-camera without microphone.
note: Manifest line 4 has RECORD_AUDIO; grep of src/ finds no audio/microphone/recording use, expo-camera plugin config sets only cameraPermission (QR scan). PLAY_STORE_LAUNCH.md flags it. Unused sensitive permission.

issue: A second intent-filter sets android:autoVerify="true" on the custom scheme "upcheckapp" (no host, not https); autoVerify only applies to http/https App Links with a verified domain, so it is a no-op that logs a manifest warning, and it duplicates the plain upcheckapp intent-filter at lines 37-42.
fix: Delete the duplicate autoVerify intent-filter (lines 43-48); keep the single custom-scheme filter.
note: Lines 43-48: second intent-filter with android:autoVerify="true" on custom scheme 'upcheckapp' (no host, not https) — autoVerify only applies to http/https App Links, so no-op + manifest warning; duplicates the scheme filter at 37-42. Also carries an invalid data-generated attr.

issue: SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW ("display over other apps") is declared in the production main manifest, but no feature draws overlays (grep of src/ finds only UI style "overlay", no canDrawOverlays/manageOverlay). This is a Play-scrutinized permission that inflates review and Data Safety surface; doc PLAY_STORE_LAUNCH.md:14 flags it but it was never removed.
fix: Delete the SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW uses-permission line from the main manifest (add tools:node="remove" if a dependency re-injects it).
note: Line 5 declares SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW; no canDrawOverlays/manageOverlay/overlay-draw code in src/. PLAY_STORE_LAUNCH.md itself notes it's likely dependency-injected and should be removed. Unused permission adding review surface.

issue: WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE (and READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE line 3) are declared without android:maxSdkVersion; they are ignored on the targetSdk 33+ this app builds against and only widen the Data Safety/storage-access surface flagged by Play.
fix: Add android:maxSdkVersion="32" to both, or remove them if scoped-storage image-picker is sufficient.
note: READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE (line 3) and WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE (line 7) declared without android:maxSdkVersion. Expo SDK (edge-to-edge deprecation → SDK 53+) sets targetSdk 34/35, so WRITE is ignored and READ superseded by granular media perms on target; only widens declared-permission surface.

issue: A live Supabase project URL (mcslntwchfucavjrrhnu.supabase.co) and publishable anon key are hardcoded as source fallbacks (l.48-49). The publishable key is client-safe by design so this is not a secret leak, but committing the concrete project identifiers as a code fallback hard-binds the repo to one project and defeats the env override for anyone building from source.
fix: Remove the string fallbacks and fail loudly (throw) when EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY are unset, keeping identifiers env-only.
note: app.config.ts:48-49 hardcode live Supabase URL + sb_publishable_ anon key as env fallbacks. Publishable key is client-safe (correct), but concrete project ref (mcslntwchfucavjrrhnu, same ref cited in ARCHITECTURE.md) hard-binds the repo; env override never fails loudly. Hygiene/IMPROVEMENT.

issue: This doc states the frontend 'communicates indirectly with the backend database via the Supabase Client SDK for standard operations (Auth, CRUD)' and (lines 51-58) that the current interaction pattern is 'Direct-to-Database (Supabase)' via src/services/farmService.ts using supabase.from('farms').select('*'), with security 'relying on Supabase RLS' and NestJS as a 'future API gateway'. The actual code does the opposite: there is no frontend/src/services/ dir, no supabase.from(...) data access anywhere in frontend/src, all CRUD goes through axios modules in frontend/src/api/*.ts to the NestJS API, and ARCHITECTURE.md's central invariant is 'the client never reads/writes Postgres directly.' A dev following this doc would build a direct-to-DB client that breaks the tenant-isolation model.
fix: Delete frontend/backend.md (it describes a removed/never-shipped architecture) or rewrite it to point at docs/guides/frontend.md and the api/* → NestJS flow.
note: frontend/backend.md:8,51-58 claim frontend does direct-to-DB CRUD via src/services/farmService.ts using supabase.from('farms'). No src/services dir exists; all CRUD goes through frontend/src/api/*.ts (axios apiClient) to NestJS. docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:63-64 states the invariant 'client never reads/writes Postgres directly' — backend.md directly contradicts it. Supabase SDK in src/ is auth-only (lib/supabase.ts, authStore.ts). Stale/misleading doc, not code — no runtime impact, so severity downgraded.

issue: Production build profile has no autoIncrement and cli has no appVersionSource; the committed bare-workflow build.gradle:96 pins versionCode 1, so every production AAB ships versionCode 1 and Play rejects the second upload ("Version code 1 has already been used").
fix: Add "appVersionSource": "remote" under cli (or "autoIncrement": true to the production build profile) in eas.json.
note: eas.json production profile (lines 18-23) has no autoIncrement and cli block (lines 2-3) has no appVersionSource; committed bare-workflow build.gradle:96 pins versionCode 1. EAS uses local versionCode as-is, so every production AAB ships versionCode 1 → Play rejects the second upload. Real release blocker.

issue: @react-native-picker/picker is pinned ^2.11.4 while the installed Expo SDK 54 expects 2.11.1 (frontend/web_output.log warns the project "may not work correctly"); expo doctor flags the mismatch and it risks web/native picker breakage.
fix: Pin @react-native-picker/picker to 2.11.1 to match the Expo SDK-aligned version.
note: web_output.log:4 confirms picker@2.11.4 vs expected 2.11.1; pinned ^2.11.4 at package.json:23; Picker used in MeasurementsScreen.

area: Frontend has no lint/test scripts or eslint config
issue: frontend/package.json scripts define only expo start/android/ios/web — no `test` and no `lint`. `npm test` and `npm run lint` both error 'Missing script'. There is no eslint config and no eslint dependency at all, yet frontend/src contains 6 `// eslint-disable-next-line ...` directives (e.g. react-hooks/exhaustive-deps in usePermissions.ts:82, CreatePondScreen.tsx:73) that are inert since nothing lints them. The 120 jest tests only run via ad-hoc `npx jest`.
fix: Add `"test": "jest"` and an eslint config + `"lint": "eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx"` to frontend, and wire both into CI.
note: No test/lint scripts, no eslint dep or config file; 6 inert eslint-disable directives exist. Verified.

issue: Requests queued behind an in-flight refresh are retried via apiClient(originalRequest) without setting originalRequest._retry = true (only the refresh initiator sets it at client.ts:86). If such a retried request immediately 401s again (token revoked in the window), it re-enters the response interceptor and triggers another refresh instead of failing out, since the _retry guard at client.ts:64 is not set on it.
fix: Set originalRequest._retry = true before returning apiClient(originalRequest) in the queued branch.
note: Queued retry at client.ts:80 omits _retry; a re-401 would start one extra refresh instead of failing out. Real but low impact.

issue: The 'Offline-first measurement queue (capture works with no signal, PRD §8.A)' module is dead code — measurementSync.enqueue/flush/pendingCount are never imported anywhere in src. MeasurementsScreen.handleSubmit calls measurementsApi.create directly (frontend/src/screens/measurements/MeasurementsScreen.tsx:104) and on a network error only shows a 'no internet' alert and discards the reading. The claimed offline-first measurement capture is unimplemented at the UI; readings taken offline are lost, not queued.
fix: Route MeasurementsScreen saves through measurementSync.enqueue (or saveRecord) and call measurementSync.flush() on reconnect in OfflineIndicator, or delete the unused module.
note: measurementSync has zero importers; MeasurementsScreen.handleSubmit calls measurementsApi.create directly and on network error (line 118-122) alerts and returns, discarding the reading. Offline-first unimplemented at UI.

area: Inert lint suppressions hide stale-closure risk
issue: react-hooks/exhaustive-deps is disabled here and in 4 screens (CreatePondScreen, ReferenceScreen, LunarScreen, TransactionsScreen) — the exact rule that catches stale-closure bugs — but with no eslint configured the suppressions do nothing and the rule never actually runs to verify the deps are safe.
fix: Configure eslint with eslint-plugin-react-hooks, then justify or remove each exhaustive-deps disable.
note: usePermissions.ts:82 exhaustive-deps disable confirmed; no eslint configured so it never runs. Same pattern in the 4 other screens.

issue: All five non-English locales carry engines.lunar.stepPeak/stepPre/stepPost/stepNoHandling and duplicate engines.routine.phase_* keys that no code references (grep of frontend/src finds zero usages) and that the English locale (en/engines.ts) does not even contain. They appear to be an abandoned attempt to localize the lunar playbook (which is still rendered raw from the backend), leaving translated strings that never display and 14 keys that break structural parity with the en base.
fix: Either wire the lunar playbook to these keys (see LunarScreen finding) or delete stepPeak/stepPre/stepPost/stepNoHandling and the routine.phase_* duplicates from all locale files.
note: stepPeak/Pre/Post/NoHandling present in hi/ta/te/bn/or, absent in en; phase_ block duplicated in non-en routine section; unreferenced in src. Real dead-key/parity issue but no display/runtime impact, so LOW.

area: content — placeholder legal copy shipped in-app
issue: LEGAL_META fields are unfilled bracket placeholders (contactEmail '[support@yourdomain.com]', address '[Registered office address]', governingLaw '[State], India', effectiveDate '[Effective date]', lastUpdated '[Last updated date]', privacyUrl/termsUrl/deletionUrl). They are interpolated straight into the Privacy Policy and Terms blocks rendered by LegalScreen (content.ts:33 'Effective [Effective date]. Last updated [Last updated date].', :114 'requests: [support@yourdomain.com]. Upcheck Technologies, [Registered office address].', :182 'Contact: [support@yourdomain.com].'), so users literally read the square-bracket placeholders as the legal text.
fix: Populate LEGAL_META with the real company email, registered address, jurisdiction, dates and hosted policy URLs before any release build.
note: LEGAL_META bracket placeholders (contactEmail/address/dates/URLs/governingLaw) interpolated into rendered PRIVACY_POLICY/TERMS text at content.ts:33,114,123,182. Users would read the brackets in a release build.

issue: Reset-password validation only checks password.length < 8, weaker than the enforced signup policy (passwordPolicy: upper+lower+digit+special), so a user can set a password via reset that the Register form would reject.
fix: Reuse passwordPolicyError(password) here so reset and signup enforce the same rule.
note: ResetPasswordScreen:52 checks only password.length<8, while RegisterScreen:41 uses passwordPolicyError (upper+lower+digit+special+len8). Reset uses supabase.auth.updateUser directly, bypassing the backend SignupDto regex, so a reset password like 'password' is accepted where signup rejects it. Real inconsistency; LOW correct.

issue: Login-failure alert bodies are hardcoded English while only the titles use t(): 'The server did not return a session. Please try again.' (:111), fallback 'Truecaller verification failed. Please try again.' (:120), and 'Could not reach the server. Please try again.' (:125). Non-English users hit English error messages during sign-in failures.
fix: Add auth.* keys for these three messages to all 6 locales and pass them through t().
note: Bodies hardcoded English at :111, :120, :125 while titles use t('auth.loginFailed')/t('auth.networkError'). Non-English users get English failure text. Confirmed MEDIUM.

issue: handleCalculate calls calculatorsApi.calculateCultivationPerformance and stores perfRes.data as results.perf, but the results JSX only renders fcr/adg/sr/productivity — perf is never displayed, so the extra network round-trip (and its fr computation at line 99) is dead.
fix: Render the perf fields or drop the calculateCultivationPerformance call from Promise.all.
note: perfRes.data stored as results.perf (line 113) and the fr computation runs at line 99, but results JSX (237-266) renders only fcr/adg/sr/productivity — perf never read. Dead API call. LOW correct.

issue: The concentration-adjusted amount uses clientCalc = (pondVolume*ppm)/(conc*10000). Backend (correct) amount = pondVolume*ppm/1000 kg of active ingredient, so a product at conc% needs pondVolume*ppm/(10*conc) kg. Dividing by conc*10000 is 1000x too small — at conc=100 it shows 0.01 kg where 10 kg is required, and the printed formula string (line 149) is likewise wrong. A farmer following it grossly under-doses a treatment.
fix: Change the divisor to conc*10 (clientCalc = pondVolume*ppm/(conc*10)) and update the displayed formula text accordingly.
note: Backend amountKg=(area*depth*ppm)/1000 (service.ts:262) = active kg at 100%. Product at conc% needs pondVolume*ppm/(10*conc); client divides by conc*10000 (line 59) = 1000x too small. At conc=100 shows 0.01 vs 10 kg. Displayed formula string (line 149) also wrong. HIGH correct.

issue: Stocking date is a free-text Input with no validation and no date picker (inconsistent with PondSetupScreen's CalendarPicker); a malformed date string is sent straight to cropsApi.create.
fix: Replace the stockingDate Input with the CalendarPicker component and validate it parses to a real date before submit.
note: stockingDate is free-text Input (99-105), no format validation (handleSave only checks name+stockingCount); PondSetupScreen uses CalendarPicker. Real gap, but field is prefilled valid via todayLocalISODate() so only edited-to-malformed input escapes — LOW.

issue: The button labelled 'Record harvest' (cycles.btnRecordHarvest) navigates to 'HarvestHistory', the read-only history view, not the 'HarvestLog' recording screen.
fix: Change navigation.navigate('HarvestHistory', …) to navigation.navigate('HarvestLog', { pondId: cycle.pondId, pondName, cropId: cycle.id }).
note: Button label cycles.btnRecordHarvest navigates to 'HarvestHistory' (line 134), the list screen; the record form is 'HarvestLog' (RootNavigator:311, used directly by PondDashboard:399). Nuance: HarvestHistoryScreen has a FAB to reach HarvestLog, so recording is still reachable in 2 taps — mislabeled primary CTA, not a dead end. MEDIUM defensible.

issue: fetchCycle() catches errors with only console.error; on API failure isLoading becomes false while cycle stays null, and the guard `if (isLoading || !cycle) return <Text>Loading</Text>` leaves the screen stuck on "Loading..." forever with no error/retry UI.
fix: Add an error state + retry button (mirror PondDashboard's ErrorState/NetworkError pattern) instead of the bare loading text.
note: fetchCycle catches with only console.error (line 25-26); finally sets isLoading=false while cycle stays null, so guard `if (isLoading || !cycle)` (line 60) renders loading text forever with no error/retry. Confirmed MEDIUM.

issue: The Lunar Molt 'What to do now' playbook is English-only in every language. step.text (:191), playbook.headline (:156), playbook.phaseLabel (:155), playbook.note (:168) and risk.band (:142) all come from the backend as hardcoded English (e.g. lunar.service.ts:281 'Pre-molt — build reserves', :331 'Reduce feed 15–30% …') and are rendered without any t() call, so Telugu/Tamil/etc users get English advice on a core decision-engine screen.
fix: Return stable string codes from the lunar service and map them to t('engines.lunar.*') keys in LunarScreen, or localize the playbook text server-side by Accept-Language.
note: lunar.service.ts hardcodes English phaseLabel/headline/note/step-text/band; LunarScreen renders all raw (155,156,191,168,142) with no t().

issue: When a scanned QR has a valid prefix but the user lookup fails (not found), resolveUser's catch never resets `scanned`, so onBarcode's `if (scanned) return` blocks all further scans and the camera silently stops working until the user toggles modes.
fix: In resolveUser's catch (or after the not-found Alert), call setScanned(false) so the camera can rescan.
note: resolveUser catch (58-63) never setScanned(false); after not-found scanned stays true and onBarcode's guard blocks all rescans until mode toggle.

issue: During pendingFarmSetup this screen (the forced first-run route) has no header, back, or logout affordance, so a freshly-registered owner cannot sign out or switch accounts without force-quitting the app.
fix: Show a logout/sign-out action in the setup header when pendingFarmSetup is true.
note: pendingFarmSetup screen has only a text setup header and form, no logout/sign-out affordance on the forced first-run route.

issue: load() swallows all API errors and sets members to [], so a network/server failure renders the 'No members' empty state — the owner is misled into thinking the roster is empty with no error or retry.
fix: Track an error state and render ErrorState/NetworkError with retry when the members request fails.
note: load() catch swallows every error and sets members=[], rendering the empty state with no error/retry.

area: finance — pond id must be typed manually
issue: The add-expense form requires formPondId as a raw text Input (placeholderPondId) and it is a required field, so a farmer has to hand-type a pond UUID to save an expense even though the screen already receives cropId/pondName from route params. Effectively unusable.
fix: Replace the pondId text Input with a pond picker sourced from the crop/farm, or derive pondId from route params.
note: formPondId is a required raw text Input; route params give only cropId/pondName, so pondId must be hand-typed and cannot be derived.

area: finance — invalid date crashes save silently
issue: formDate is a free-text field. handleSubmit builds the payload with new Date(formDate).toISOString() at line 126, OUTSIDE the try block (which starts at 133), after setIsSubmitting(true). A malformed date makes toISOString() throw RangeError: the save spinner stays stuck true forever and no error is shown to the user.
fix: Validate the parsed date (isNaN(new Date(formDate).getTime())) before submit, or move payload construction inside the try/catch.
note: payload w/ new Date(formDate).toISOString() at :126 is outside try (:133) after setIsSubmitting(true); no validity check, so a bad date throws RangeError leaving spinner stuck.

issue: Lines 259-261 are a no-op ternary (Alert.prompt !== undefined ? undefined : undefined) that does nothing.
fix: Delete lines 259-261.
note: Lines 259-261 are a literal no-op ternary (Alert.prompt !== undefined ? undefined : undefined); dead code.

issue: The entire screen has no useTranslation/t() — every string ('Harvest Plans', 'Mark Complete', 'Loading harvest plans…', STATUS_CONFIG labels Planned/Completed/Cancelled at lines 30/35/40, all form labels, all alert titles/messages) is hard-coded English. The app is otherwise fully i18n'd (Tamil/Hindi), so this screen never translates.
fix: Import useTranslation and route every literal through t() with keys, as sibling screens do.
note: No useTranslation import in file; STATUS_CONFIG labels (30/35/40), 'Harvest Plans' (378), all form/alert strings hardcoded English while sibling screens use t(). Same defect as id 86.

issue: This live, navigable screen (routed from CycleDetailScreen and PondDashboardScreen, RootNavigator.tsx:384) has no useTranslation/t() at all — all 39 user-facing strings are hardcoded English literals: status labels 'Planned'/'Completed'/'Cancelled', alerts ('Mark as Completed', 'Delete Plan', 'Failed to load/complete/delete harvest plan', 'Validation Error'), form labels ('Planned Harvest Date', 'Target Weight (kg)', 'Expected Price per kg (₹)', 'Notes'), and empty state ('No Harvest Plans'). Breaks the README 'in 6 languages' claim for the entire harvest-planning feature; Telugu/Tamil/Bengali/Odia/Hindi users see raw English here.
fix: Add const { t } = useTranslation() and route every literal through t('harvestPlans.*'), adding the keys to all 6 locale files.
note: Duplicate of id 73 at line 75; screen has zero i18n. Language-count claim unverified but i18n absence is real; HIGH defensible for a fully un-localized live screen.

issue: promptCompleteValues promises 'You will be asked for actual harvest weight and price per kg' but never collects input — onConfirm is hard-coded to actualWeightKg:'0', actualPricePerKg:'0', so completing any harvest plan always POSTs zero weight/price (data loss or backend rejection). The core 'Mark Complete' action cannot record real values.
fix: Replace the Alert-only flow with a real modal form (two TextInputs) and pass the entered numbers to harvestPlansApi.complete.
note: Line 84 onConfirm hard-codes '0'/'0'; handleComplete sends Number('0')=0 for weight and price on every completion despite the prompt promising to collect them.

area: inventory — stock management is a stub
issue: Add stock, reduce stock (lines 45-46), edit item (line 53) and stock history (line 176) are all 'coming soon' Alert stubs, even though inventoryApi.adjustStock and inventoryApi.update exist on the backend. Inventory items can be viewed but never adjusted or edited from the app.

note: handleAdjustStock (45-46), handleEdit (53), stock history (176) are all comingSoon alerts though inventoryApi.adjustStock/update exist; incomplete feature, not a crash but claim accurate.

area: inventory — Add Item is a dead button
issue: Both the FAB (line 243) and the empty-state action (line 237) only fire Alert.alert(t('inventory.addItem'), t('inventory.addItemComingSoon')). There is no way to create an inventory item from the app despite inventoryApi.create existing.
