note: FAB (243) and empty-state action (237) both only Alert comingSoon; inventoryApi.create exists so no create path from app.

area: logs — missing range validation on two fields
issue: Nitrate (line 112) and Hardness (line 115) ParameterInput are rendered without a parameterKey, while every other water-quality field passes one. Those two fields get no range/warning validation, unlike the rest of the form.
fix: Add parameterKey="nitrate" and parameterKey="hardness" to match the other ParameterInput fields.
note: nitrate (112) and hardness (115) ParameterInput omit parameterKey; all 10 sibling inputs pass one, so those two get no range validation.

issue: Dashboard summary fetch failure is swallowed (console.error) leaving summary null, so the 'No farm data / Create farm' CTA is shown even when the user already has farms and only the request failed.
fix: Distinguish a fetch error from a truly-empty result and show a retry state instead of the create-farm CTA on error.
note: catch at 48 only console.errors, summary stays null → renders create-farm CTA (163-183); legit empty case returns all-zeros non-null, so CTA is effectively error-only.

issue: load() catch sets ponds to [], so a network failure renders the 'No ponds / Create farm' empty state rather than an error — the farmer is pushed to create a farm they already have.
fix: Add an error branch with a retry button distinct from the genuine no-ponds empty state.
note: Line 49-50 catch{ setPonds([]) } with no error state; network failure renders ponds.length===0 empty state (line 83) identical to genuine no-ponds. Real UX defect.

issue: The timestamp row concatenates a literal ' at ' between date and time ({date} at {time}), which is not run through t() and won't localize.
fix: Move the joiner into a translation key (e.g. t('common.dateAtTime', { date, time })).
note: Line 164 concatenates literal ' at ' between toLocaleDateString and toLocaleTimeString, outside t(); not localized.

issue: handleSaveStep creates a pond then a crop in two non-atomic API calls; if cropsApi.create fails after pondsApi.create succeeds, the pond is orphaned and pressing save again re-creates a duplicate pond (non-idempotent write).
fix: Keep the created pondId in state and skip pond re-creation on retry, or have the backend create pond+crop in one transactional endpoint.
note: pondsApi.create (169) then cropsApi.create (189) non-atomic; catch (210) only Alerts and keeps no pondId, so crop failure leaves orphan pond and retry re-runs create. Orphan is certain; duplicate-pond depends on backend name uniqueness.

issue: handleSave only validates namePrefix and depth; a rectangular/raceway/circular pond can be submitted with blank length/width/diameter (computedArea 0), relying entirely on the server to reject.
fix: Require the relevant dimension fields (length+width, or diameter) per geometry before allowing save.
note: handleSave validates only namePrefix + depthM (110-121); length/width/diameter passed undefined when blank (131-133), server-only rejection.

issue: DOC is computed 0-based here (calculateDOC returns floor(diff)) and in CycleDetailScreen, but PondSetupScreen.dayOfCulture is 1-based (Math.max(1, diff+1)); the same pond shows a DOC that differs by one across screens.
fix: Pick one convention (stocking day = Day 1) and share a single dayOfCulture helper across all three screens.
note: PondSetup dayOfCulture=Math.max(1,diff+1) 1-based (line 30); PondDashboard calculateDOC=floor(diff) (310) and CycleDetail calculateDOC=floor(diff) (68) 0-based. Same pond differs by one on stocking day.

issue: getDimensionHistory failure is swallowed to an empty list, so a network/server error renders the 'No changes yet' empty state indistinguishably from a genuinely empty history.
fix: Add an error/retry state separate from the empty state.
note: Line 39 .catch(()=>setRows([])); loading:false with empty rows renders 'No changes yet' indistinguishable from real error.

issue: The offlineSync, pushNotifications and emailAlerts toggles only write to AsyncStorage; no code anywhere reads these keys, so they are non-functional — e.g. turning off 'Push Notifications' does nothing to actually stop notifications.
fix: Wire each toggle to its real effect (push register/unregister, email-pref API, sync gate) or remove the toggles until backed.
note: grep of offlineSync/pushNotifications/emailAlerts outside SettingsScreen returns only i18n locale strings; no getItem reader of these keys anywhere. Toggles write AsyncStorage only, non-functional.

issue: The Shop lists products with price/sale-price/stock but product cards have no onPress and there is no detail, cart, or purchase action anywhere on the screen — it is a read-only catalog presented as a 'Shop'. Tapping a product does nothing (navigation dead-end).
fix: Either add a product-detail/enquiry action on tap, or rename the surface to 'Catalog' to match what it does.
note: renderProductItem (ShopScreen.tsx:155+) has no onPress; onPress only on category chips (142) and back button (226). Read-only catalog is accurate, but it's a scope/UX gap, not a defect — LOW/IMPROVEMENT.

issue: computeDOC does new Date(stockingDate) where stockingDate is 'YYYY-MM-DD', which JS parses as UTC midnight, then subtracts local now and floors the ms difference. For IST (+5:30) and other positive offsets the day count is off by one in early-morning local time (and negative on the stocking day before ~05:30 local). DOC (days of culture) drives feed-rate and growth advice. The repo already has toLocalISODate (utils/localDate.ts) created to avoid exactly this UTC-vs-local bug, but computeDOC ignores it.
fix: Compute the difference from local calendar dates (build both dates from local Y/M/D, or diff todayLocalISODate() vs stockingDate), not raw UTC-parsed timestamps.
note: Reproduced in TZ=Asia/Kolkata: new Date('YYYY-MM-DD') is UTC midnight diffed vs local now → DOC=6 vs true 7 at 03:00 IST. localDate.ts helper exists and is ignored. MEDIUM correct.

issue: roleForFarm returns null for a farm the user OWNS: backend GET /farm-members/mine (listMine) only returns explicit farm_member rows, and farms.service.create never inserts an owner membership row (backend/src/farms/farms.service.ts:84-102), so the farm creator has no row. usePermissions then denies canViewFinancials, canManageOperations, canManageMembers and all owner actions to the owner on their own farm (hidden at HomeScreen.tsx:89/222, PondDashboardScreen.tsx:456). The doc comment at membershipStore.ts:9-10 claims 'Owner is the safe default for the legacy single-user case' but the code returns null. Backend getRoleOnFarm correctly returns 'owner' via farm.userId (backend/src/farm-access/farm-access.service.ts:71); the frontend has no such fallback.
fix: Inject 'owner' for farms the user owns — have backend listMine also return owned farms as role 'owner', or merge owned-farm ids into memberships and return 'owner' from roleForFarm.
note: farms.service.create (84-101) saves only the farm; owner farm_member row is backfilled only by one-time migration 1780300700000. Every farm created afterward has no owner row → listMine omits it → roleForFarm null → financials/ops/members hidden from owner (Home 89/222, PondDashboard 456). Frontend lacks backend's farm.userId fallback.

issue: drainQueue counts transient failures toward MAX_SYNC_RETRIES. replayQueuedOp returns 'retry' for network/timeout, 5xx, and 401/403 (recordSync.ts:75-77); only 400/422 return 'failed'. Since poison ops (400/422) are parked immediately and never ping-pong, the retry cap only ever fires on transient failures of VALID records. During an extended server outage or 5 flaky reconnects, legitimate queued records are parked in failedOperations and surfaced as 'records need attention', contradicting the SYNC-3 comment (syncStore.ts:12-15) that the cap exists for poison ops.
fix: Only increment retryCount for 'failed'/unexpected outcomes; do not consume the cap on pure connectivity failures (outcome 'retry' with no HTTP status).
note: replayQueuedOp parks 400/422 immediately as 'failed'; only transient 'retry' outcomes consume MAX_SYNC_RETRIES, so cap only bites valid records during outage — contradicts SYNC-3 poison-op rationale. Real, but records are surfaced+user-retryable (no data loss), so MEDIUM not HIGH.

issue: Doc states "...`tsc --noEmit` clean, backend lint clean." tsc is clean, but backend lint is NOT clean: eslint reports 1042 errors (exit 1). The 'backend lint clean' claim is false.
fix: Either make lint actually clean, or correct the sentence to reflect the real lint state.
note: eslint on project glob (4GB heap) = 1063 errors, exit 1; 'backend lint clean' is false (tsc claim is true). Doc-only inaccuracy in launch doc — LOW.

area: Auth mirror trigger — no DELETE sync
issue: The auth->public.users mirror installs only INSERT (on_auth_user_created) and UPDATE (on_auth_user_updated) triggers. public.users.id has no FK to auth.users, so deleting a user directly in Supabase (dashboard/admin GDPR delete, bypassing the app's deleteAccount) removes auth.users but leaves an orphaned public.users row plus all their farms/ponds/crops/logs forever, and a later re-signup with the same email hits the UNIQUE(email) constraint inside the trigger INSERT (which conflicts only on id, baseline line 8/94) and fails.
fix: Add an AFTER DELETE ON auth.users trigger that runs DELETE FROM public.users WHERE id = OLD.id.
note: public.users has UNIQUE(email)+PK(id), no FK to auth.users (baseline:8); setup SQL installs only INSERT/UPDATE triggers with ON CONFLICT(id). Direct auth delete orphans public.users+farms; same-email re-signup gets new id → email UNIQUE violation in trigger INSERT → signup fails. MEDIUM correct.

area: Dependencies / two libs same job, one unused
issue: @getbrevo/brevo SDK is declared as a prod dependency but email.service.ts calls the Brevo REST endpoint directly with fetch and never imports the SDK; the SDK is unused dead weight alongside the also-unused nodemailer.
fix: Remove @getbrevo/brevo from backend/package.json dependencies.
note: @getbrevo/brevo (line 29) imported nowhere in backend; email.service.ts sends via Brevo REST, SDK is dead weight.

issue: npm audit --omit=dev on installed backend tree reports 18 HIGH vulns in the prod runtime, including @nestjs/core injection (GHSA-36xv-jgw5-4q75), @nestjs/platform-express (vulnerable multer + path-to-regexp ReDoS), and ws uninitialized-memory disclosure (GHSA-58qx-3vcg-4xpx); all have fixAvailable.

note: npm audit --omit=dev: 18 high (incl. @nestjs/core path-to-regexp, @nestjs/platform-express multer+path-to-regexp, ws uninit-memory), all fixAvailable=true. GHSA for @nestjs/core is path-to-regexp ReDoS not 'injection' but count/impact hold.

fix: Remove argon2 from backend/package.json (bcrypt is the actual hasher).
note: argon2 (line 42) imported nowhere; bcrypt is the actual hasher. Unused native build.

issue: nodemailer is declared as a prod dependency but is never imported anywhere in the repo (email.service.ts sends via raw fetch to the Brevo HTTP API); dead weight shipped in the production bundle.
fix: Remove nodemailer from backend/package.json dependencies.

fix: Remove ua-parser-js from backend/package.json (or if device parsing is intended, wire it up and run npm audit fix).
note: ua-parser-js (line 61) imported nowhere; audit confirms moderate ReDoS advisory. Unused attack surface.

fix: Remove zxcvbn and @types/zxcvbn from backend/package.json.

issue: liveBriefing (endpoint GET alert-center live briefing) sequentially awaits pondContext.getContext(p.id, userId) inside a for-of over all active ponds; each getContext fires ~6 DB queries, so an N-pond farm serializes ~6N round-trips.
fix: Replace the for loop with await Promise.all(mine.map(p => getContext(p.id, userId))) to fan out per-pond context fetches.
note: liveBriefing lines 145-152: for-of with per-pond `await getContext` serializes all active-pond context fetches. Note: naive Promise.all fan-out would hammer the max=5 pool; batch it.

area: Ops — silent misconfig fallback in production
issue: When DATABASE_URL is unset the code logs a warning via console.error and silently falls back to a localhost Postgres connection even in production, masking the misconfiguration behind a generic connection error instead of failing fast at boot.
fix: Throw at startup when NODE_ENV==='production' and DATABASE_URL is missing, rather than falling back to localhost.
note: app.module.ts:75-77 console.error then proceeds with url:undefined -> localhost defaults; no fail-fast in production. LOW correct.

area: Concurrency — single-use backup code double-use / lost update
issue: verifyCodeOrBackup reads user.backupCodes, splices the matched code in memory, and saves the whole array (findOneBy -> splice -> save). Two concurrent 2FA challenges submitting the SAME backup code both read the array, both bcrypt.compare true, both splice+save -> the single-use recovery code is accepted twice. Concurrent logins using two DIFFERENT backup codes also lose one consumption because save() overwrites the entire array with each request's stale copy, restoring an already-used code.
fix: Consume the code atomically: run the compare+removal inside a transaction with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the user row (or store hashes in a backup_codes table with a unique row deleted via a single guarded DELETE returning affected>0).
note: two-factor.service.ts:178-185 findOneBy->splice->save with no lock/transaction; same-code double-use and different-code lost-update both real.

issue: The only global exception filter is `@Catch(QueryFailedError)` and logs to console only; all non-DB exceptions and 500s bypass it (handled by Nest's default logger with no route/context) and nothing is reported to any external tracker, so production backend errors are invisible for a pre-launch service.
fix: Add a catch-all `@Catch()` ExceptionFilter that logs request context and calls Sentry.captureException, and register it globally in main.ts.
note: main.ts:50 registers only TypeORMExceptionFilter (@Catch(QueryFailedError)); no catch-all filter and no Sentry/tracker in repo.

area: Concurrency — double cycle close / duplicate harvest records
issue: closeCycle unconditionally sets status='completed' with no precheck that the crop is still open, and harvests.create (harvests.service.ts:23) triggers it for every 'full' harvest. A double-submitted or concurrently-replayed full-harvest creates two harvest rows and closes the cycle twice, double-counting yield/revenue in reports and P&L.
fix: Make close idempotent: update with a guard `WHERE id=:id AND status <> 'completed'` and throw ConflictException when affected===0, so the second close (and its duplicate harvest) is rejected.
note: closeCycle:218 unconditional update to status='completed'; harvests.service.ts:23 calls it on every 'full' harvest with no idempotency guard.

area: Concurrency — two active cycles per pond (check-then-act, no constraint)
issue: create() checks pond.activeCycleId then, later (line 78), sets it. There is no DB constraint enforcing one active crop per pond, so two concurrent CreateCycle requests for the same pond both read activeCycleId=null, both pass the ConflictException guard, both create 'active' crops, and both call pond.update (last write wins). The pond ends with two active cycles, breaking DOC/stocking-density/P&L invariants.
fix: Add a partial unique index (one crop with status='active' per pond_id) so the second insert fails, or wrap the check+set in a transaction that locks the pond row (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE).
note: create() check at :25-32, set at :77-83; no unique index in crop.entity.ts enforcing one active cycle per pond; check-then-act race real.

issue: commonNames/symptoms/preventionMeasures/treatmentRecommendations/imageUrls/photoUrls are all @IsArray() with no @ArrayMaxSize, allowing oversized arrays to be stored.
fix: Add @ArrayMaxSize(50) plus @MaxLength on each element to every array field in this DTO.
note: create-disease.dto.ts arrays commonNames/symptoms/preventionMeasures/treatmentRecommendations/imageUrls/photoUrls (and photoUrls in CreateDiseaseRecordDto) have @IsArray with no @ArrayMaxSize/@MaxLength.

issue: getFarmIdsWithCapability loops over every accessible farm id and calls getRoleOnFarm(userId, farmId) per iteration, each doing 1-2 DB queries (farm_members findOne + farms findOne fallback); called by transactions list (transactions.service.ts:41) and reports, so query count scales linearly with farm count.
fix: Load all farm_members rows for the user once and resolve roles from that map instead of a per-farm getRoleOnFarm call.
note: getFarmIdsWithCapability (lines 120-126) loops accessibleIds and calls getRoleOnFarm per farm, each doing membersRepo.findOne + fallback farmsRepo.findOne; getAccessibleFarmIds already loaded the member rows, so this re-queries redundantly. Called by transactions.service.ts:41. Real N+1.

issue: name (and other string fields at lines 19/27/50/57) have no @MaxLength; a 1MB farm name is accepted and stored, and re-served in every farm list.
fix: Add @MaxLength(120) on name and reasonable caps on the other string fields.
note: name (line 16) and other string fields have no @MaxLength — confirmed. But default ~100kb body limit caps input, so the '1MB name' figure is overstated; genuine missing field cap, LOW.

issue: boundary: {latitude,longitude}[] is @IsArray() with no size cap and no @ValidateNested, so a huge coordinate array is accepted, validated loosely, and stored as JSON on the farm row.
fix: Add @ArrayMaxSize(1000) and @ValidateNested({each:true}) with a typed coordinate class.
note: boundary (lines 54-55) is @IsArray with no @ArrayMaxSize and no @ValidateNested — confirmed. DoS bounded by default ~100kb body limit, so HIGH is overstated; nested objects pass unvalidated into stored JSON. MEDIUM.

issue: cropId column has no @Index, but it is the query filter in feeding-tray-checks.service.ts:60 (WHERE ftc.cropId) and pond-context.service.ts (trayRepo.findOne WHERE cropId ORDER BY checkDate), unindexed on a per-request hot path.
fix: Add @Index() to the cropId column of feeding_tray_checks.
note: cropId column (line 17) has no @Index; ManyToOne/FK does not auto-index in Postgres. Used as filter predicate in feeding-tray-checks.service.ts:60 (andWhere ftc.cropId) and pond-context findOne. Legitimate missing index, MEDIUM.

issue: priceBands array feeds an economics computation but has no @ArrayMaxSize; a large array forces an unbounded compute loop per request.
fix: Add @ArrayMaxSize(50) to priceBands.
note: priceBands (line 33) has @ValidateNested + @Type but no @ArrayMaxSize — confirmed. Unbounded loop is real but bounded by ~100kb body limit (thousands of entries max), so MEDIUM overstated. LOW.

issue: Only 3 of 88 DTOs use @MaxLength and the global ValidationPipe sets no default length or array bound, so every unlisted string/array field is an unbounded-input surface; whitelist:true only strips unknown keys, it does not cap sizes.
fix: Adopt a shared base DTO or a project rule adding @MaxLength/@ArrayMaxSize to every string/array field; audit the remaining 85 DTOs.
note: Verified: 3 DTO files use @MaxLength, 1 uses @ArrayMaxSize, out of 88 DTO files; ValidationPipe (main.ts:42-48) sets whitelist/transform only, no default length/array caps. whitelist strips unknown keys, does not cap sizes. Systemic observation accurate. LOW.

issue: createBatch (offline-sync ingest) processes items in a sequential for loop; each item's create() re-runs pondsService.findOne ownership check + deriveDoc + dictionary.validate, plus a duplicate findOne in the loop, so a batch of N readings for the same pond issues ~4N sequential queries with repeated identical ownership lookups.
fix: Resolve pond ownership once per distinct pondId across the batch (cache the findOne result) instead of re-verifying inside every create().
note: createBatch loops sequentially; create() re-runs pondsService.findOne ownership + dictionary.validate + deriveDoc per item, plus redundant findOne at line 122. Real per-batch repeated ownership lookups.

issue: images: string[] has @IsArray()/@IsString({each:true}) but no @ArrayMaxSize, so a request can carry an unbounded number of unbounded strings that are persisted to the row.
fix: Add @ArrayMaxSize(20) and @MaxLength(500,{each:true}).
note: images has no @ArrayMaxSize/@MaxLength, persisted to jsonb. Real, but no custom body limit means Express default ~100KB caps worst-case payload -> MEDIUM not HIGH.

issue: boundary array on pond creation has no @ArrayMaxSize and no element validation; unbounded polygon points can be posted and persisted.
fix: Add @ArrayMaxSize(1000) and @ValidateNested({each:true}).
note: boundary has only @IsArray; no @ArrayMaxSize and no @ValidateNested (element shape unvalidated), persisted to jsonb col (pond.entity.ts:143). ~100KB default body cap bounds DoS -> MEDIUM.

issue: pondId column has no @Index, but getDimensionHistory (ponds.service.ts:374) queries findAndCount WHERE pondId ORDER BY changedAt on this table, forcing a full scan as history grows.
fix: Add @Index(['pondId','changedAt']) (or @Index() on pondId) to pond_dimension_history.
note: pond_dimension_history.pond_id has FK only (Postgres does not auto-index FKs) and no @Index; getDimensionHistory does findAndCount WHERE pondId ORDER BY changedAt (ponds.service.ts:384). Confirmed missing index.

issue: DeleteAccountDto (backend/src/auth/dto/delete-account.dto.ts) defines an optional password field intended for re-auth before this destructive action, but deleteMe() takes no @Body and never verifies a password or re-checks the session — any live session token silently permanently deletes the account with no confirmation step server-side.
fix: Either accept @Body() DeleteAccountDto and verify the password/recent-login via supabaseAuthService before deleting, or delete the unused DeleteAccountDto to avoid implying a guard that does not exist.
note: DeleteAccountDto (optional password) exists but deleteMe() takes no @Body and does no password/re-auth check before destructive delete. Confirmed LOW.

issue: deleteAccount only runs DELETE FROM users + DELETE FROM profiles and relies on ON DELETE CASCADE, but the credit_ledgers table (dealer name, principal, interest, repaid amounts, keyed by user_id) has NO foreign key at all — the migration explicitly states "No foreign keys (user-scoped)" (backend/src/migrations/1780300500000-CreateCreditLedgers.ts:4, entity backend/src/credit/credit-ledger.entity.ts:21). So every deleted user's financial/debt records survive indefinitely as orphaned rows, contradicting ACCOUNT_DELETION.md:12 ("permanently deletes... all farm data you own") and PRIVACY_POLICY.md:42 ("permanently removes your account and owned data").
fix: Add `await manager.query('DELETE FROM credit_ledgers WHERE user_id = $1', [userId])` inside the deletion transaction (or add a user_id FK with ON DELETE CASCADE to credit_ledgers).
note: deleteAccount deletes only users+profiles; credit_ledgers has NO FK (entity + migration confirm) and is never deleted -> orphaned financial rows survive. Confirmed HIGH.

issue: Supabase auth-user deletion is wrapped in a try/catch that only logs on failure, so the endpoint returns 204 and the frontend clears the session even when the auth identity was NOT removed. The local users row is already gone, but the surviving auth.users record still permits sign-in; the next login updates auth.users (last_sign_in_at), firing the on_auth_user_updated trigger (supabase_setup.sql:114) which re-INSERTs a fresh empty public.users row. Result: data wiped but the account is still signable — violating the doc promise that deletion is "immediate and irreversible" and removes the "authentication identity" (docs/legal/ACCOUNT_DELETION.md).
fix: Delete the Supabase auth user FIRST and let its failure propagate (throw) so the request fails before local data is wiped, or re-throw on auth deletion failure so the client learns the account was not fully deleted.
note: Supabase deleteUser wrapped in try/catch that only logs, endpoint returns 204 after local wipe; on_auth_user_updated trigger (AFTER UPDATE auth.users) re-INSERTs public.users on next sign-in. Account still signable. Confirmed HIGH.

issue: register uses @Body() body: { token: string } — an inline TS type, not a class, so the global ValidationPipe skips it entirely; token is completely unvalidated (missing, huge string, array, or object) and passed straight to registerToken and stored.
fix: Introduce a RegisterTokenDto class with @IsString() @IsNotEmpty() @MaxLength(256) token and use it as the @Body type.
note: Inline type {token:string} has no class-validator metadata; ValidationPipe (whitelist/transform) cannot validate it. token flows unchecked into usersRepository.update pushToken.

area: Caching & staleness (cross-tenant read on cached endpoint)
issue: getDashboardSummary passes an arbitrary client-supplied farmId straight into the service with no farm-access check (reports.service.ts:33 also omits assertCanAccessFarm). Any authenticated user can request any farmId and read that farm's activePondsCount/totalPondsCount/lowStockAlerts/todayFeedUsage — a cross-tenant leak, and the result is then cached under dashboard_summary:<attackerUserId>:<victimFarmId>.
fix: Call this.farmAccess.assertCanAccessFarm(userId, farmId, 'VIEW_DASHBOARD') at the top of getDashboardSummary before querying or caching.
note: getDashboardSummary omits assertCanAccessFarm; countActivePonds/countTotalPonds/countLowStock/getDailyFeedUsage all query by farmId with no access check. Sibling getFinancialReport has the check. Real cross-tenant leak.

issue: getDashboardSummary caches the summary for 300s but no write path (feed record create, pond create/status change, inventory adjustment) invalidates dashboard_summary:<userId>:<farmId>. After a farmer logs feed or adds a pond, the home dashboard keeps showing stale activePondsCount/lowStockAlerts/todayFeedUsage for up to 5 minutes.
fix: On feed/pond/inventory writes for a farm, call redisService.del(`dashboard_summary:${userId}:${farmId}`) (or key the cache without userId and invalidate on write).
note: Only dashboard_summary reference is the set; no del/invalidation exists. But this is an intentional 300s TTL cache — staleness within TTL is expected, not a defect; severity overstated.

issue: notes is @IsString() @IsOptional() with no @MaxLength, allowing arbitrarily large notes blobs per sampling record.
fix: Add @MaxLength(2000) on notes.
note: notes is @IsString() @IsOptional() with no @MaxLength at line 56-58.

issue: photoUrls is @IsArray() with no @ArrayMaxSize and no per-element validation; an authenticated client can POST a multi-million-element array (and arbitrary-typed elements) that gets held in memory and persisted, OOM-ing the Node process.
fix: Add @ArrayMaxSize(20) and @IsString({each:true}) @MaxLength(500,{each:true}) like measurement's create DTO already does.
note: photoUrls has no @ArrayMaxSize/each-element validation (real gap). But no raised body limit in main.ts, so Express default 100kb cap prevents multi-million-element OOM; HIGH DoS overstated.

issue: title and description are @IsString() with no @MaxLength; a client can persist multi-MB task titles/descriptions, bloating storage and list responses. Global ValidationPipe does not enforce any default length.
fix: Add @MaxLength(200) on title and @MaxLength(2000) on description.
note: title and description (lines 29-34) are @IsString() with no @MaxLength.

issue: Water-quality alert creation failures are caught and written with raw `console.error` then swallowed (no injected Logger, no re-raise, no alerting), so critical pH/DO/ammonia alerts can silently never fire and no one is notified.
fix: Replace console.error with the injected Nest Logger, report via the crash tracker, and surface a counter/metric so silent alert-creation failures are visible.
note: Alert-creation errors caught and console.error'd then swallowed (line 148-150); WaterQualityService injects no Logger, no re-raise, no metric. Alerts can silently fail to fire.

issue: npm audit --omit=dev on installed frontend tree reports 33 vulns including 1 CRITICAL shell-quote (GHSA-w7jw-789q-3m8p, via expo tooling) and multiple HIGH @xmldom/xmldom XML-injection/DoS advisories (GHSA-wh4c-j3r5-mjhp et al) pulled in through the pinned @dhana-cs/react-native-truecaller fork's bundled @expo/config-plugins/@expo/plist subtree; all show fixAvailable.
fix: Run `npm audit fix` in frontend/, and drop or replace @dhana-cs/react-native-truecaller with an npm registry package that does not vendor an ancient @expo/config-plugins.
note: npm audit --omit=dev returns exactly 33 vulns incl. critical shell-quote and high @xmldom/xmldom, @expo/config-plugins, @expo/plist, @dhana-cs fork; all fixAvailable. Location package.json:13 correct.

issue: react-native-linear-gradient@2.8.3 is unused (no imports in frontend/src); expo-linear-gradient is the one actually used in 4 files, so this is a redundant duplicate gradient library.
fix: Remove react-native-linear-gradient from dependencies.
note: react-native-linear-gradient (line 51) has zero importers in src; expo-linear-gradient used in 4 files. Redundant.

issue: react-native-linear-gradient is a prod dependency but every gradient usage in src imports LinearGradient from expo-linear-gradient (line 38); react-native-linear-gradient has zero importers, duplicating the same job with an extra native module.
fix: Remove react-native-linear-gradient from frontend/package.json.
note: Duplicate of 148; same fact verified — no react-native-linear-gradient imports, expo-linear-gradient (line 38) is the one used.

issue: react-native-maps@1.20.1 (heavyweight native module requiring Google Maps SDK linkage) is declared as a dependency but has zero imports/MapView usages anywhere under frontend/src, adding native binary size and build config for nothing.
fix: Remove react-native-maps from dependencies.
note: react-native-maps (line 52) has no imports/MapView usage anywhere in src; heavyweight native dep for nothing.

issue: The patch-package patch hard-removes the TurboModule code path (`isTurboModuleEnabled ? require('./NativeTrueCallerSDK') : NativeModules...` replaced with a bare NativeModules lookup) in a third-party module, pinning the app to the legacy bridge; on RN 0.81 New Architecture builds this silently drops the native module and Truecaller login throws the LINKING_ERROR proxy.
fix: Replace the vendored fork with a maintained Truecaller package or add a real TurboModule spec instead of deleting the branch via patch.
note: Patch line 15 hardcodes NativeModules.TrueCallerModule, deleting the turbo branch; app.config.ts:13 newArchEnabled:true corroborates the risk. Exact device-build LINKING_ERROR outcome not runtime-verified but premise holds.

area: Concurrency — offline measurement queue lost write
issue: flush() reads the queue snapshot, awaits the batch POST (seconds on mobile), then rewrites the queue with remaining = snapshot.filter(not-ok). Any reading captured during the in-flight POST is enqueued into the current store but is silently discarded when flush overwrites the queue with its stale snapshot. enqueue() (line 29) has the same read-push-write race against a concurrent flush or a second rapid capture.
fix: After the POST, re-read the queue fresh and remove only the synced ids from it (writeQueue(freshQueue.filter(q => !syncedIds.has(q.id)))) instead of rewriting from the pre-POST snapshot; serialize flush/enqueue behind a mutex.
note: flush reads snapshot (l45), awaits POST (l48), then writeQueue(remaining from stale snapshot) at l55 — any enqueue during the await is overwritten. Real lost-write TOCTOU.

issue: A single global ErrorBoundary wraps the whole NavigationContainer, and its "Try Again" only clears `hasError` without navigating away. A render crash in any deep screen tears down the entire nav stack, and resetting re-mounts the same crashing subtree, so the user is stuck in an immediate crash loop with no route to a safe screen.
fix: On reset, reset navigation to a safe root route (e.g. call a passed `onReset` that navigates Home) instead of only clearing state, and/or add finer-grained boundaries around screen content.
note: App.tsx:115 wraps whole NavigationContainer in one ErrorBoundary; handleReset (l30) only clears state, re-mounting the same route/subtree that crashes again → loop.

issue: OfflineIndicator (top: insets.top, zIndex 999) and SyncAttentionBanner (top: insets.top, zIndex 999) are both absolutely positioned at the identical top offset; when the device is offline AND records have failed to sync, the two banners render stacked on top of each other at the same coordinates.
fix: Offset SyncAttentionBanner below the offline banner (or render only one at a time) when both conditions are active.
note: Both mounted together in ScreenWrapper (lines 54-55); container top:insets.top zIndex999 and attention top:insets.top zIndex999 — offline+failed ops overlaps them exactly.

issue: When the dashboard-summary fetch fails (offline or API error), `summary` stays null and the screen renders the first-run "You have no farms — Create your first farm" CTA. An existing owner who is offline or hits a transient API error is shown the onboarding empty state, indistinguishable from a genuinely empty account, and can be pushed to create a duplicate farm. Directly contradicts the README "offline-tolerant" claim for the primary screen.
fix: Track a separate `error` state in the catch at line 48 and render an error/retry card (not the empty CTA) when the fetch failed rather than when the account is truly empty.
note: Catch at 48-52 only console.error, summary stays null → else branch (163) shows owner 'create your first farm' CTA on any fetch failure. Real UX defect; downgraded to MEDIUM (no data loss, farm creation is a deliberate multi-step action).

issue: The dashboard useEffect fetches once on mount / selectedFarm change with no focus listener and no RefreshControl, so after logging water quality/feed the home stats stay stale with no way to manually refresh — unlike FarmsList/History screens which all offer pull-to-refresh.
fix: Wrap ScreenWrapper content refetch in a RefreshControl (or re-fetch on `navigation` focus) so the primary screen can be refreshed.
note: fetchSummary runs only in useEffect deps [selectedFarm?.id]; ScreenWrapper ScrollView has no RefreshControl and no focus listener, so stats stay stale after logging.

issue: fetchSimulations catches errors with only `console.log` and leaves the list empty, so a network/API failure renders the "Create your first simulation" empty state identical to a truly-empty list. No error surfaced to the user.
fix: Add an `error` state in the catch and render an error+retry block in place of ListEmptyComponent when the load failed.
note: Lines 22-23 catch only console.log; empty simulations renders same ListEmptyComponent as a truly-empty list. No error surfaced.

issue: Icon-only controls (e.g. the arrow-left back button here) carry no accessibilityRole/accessibilityLabel; only 15 of ~77 interactive screens set any a11y props, so a screen reader announces nothing for back buttons, list rows and action icons app-wide.
fix: Add `accessibilityRole="button"` + a translated `accessibilityLabel` to icon-only TouchableOpacity controls, starting with the shared back-button pattern.
note: Back-button TouchableOpacity lines 80-82 has no accessibilityRole/Label; icon-only control announces nothing.

issue: SimulationListScreen, FeedingTrayChecksScreen and PondDimensionHistoryScreen render FlatLists of remotely-fetched data with no RefreshControl, while ~24 sibling list/history screens all provide pull-to-refresh — inconsistent gesture affordance across the app.
fix: Add a RefreshControl bound to the existing fetch function on these three FlatLists to match the rest of the app.
note: Confirmed none of the 3 named screens import RefreshControl while 29 sibling screens do — inconsistent affordance.

area: Caching & staleness (in-memory store not cleared on logout)
issue: logout() never resets the in-memory notificationStore. notifications/unreadCount/unreadCriticalCount of user A persist after logout; on a shared device user B sees user A's notification list and unread badge (pond names, cycle IDs, bodies) until a refetch overwrites them.
fix: In logout() call useNotificationStore.getState().clearAll().
note: clearAll() has zero callers repo-wide; logout() (312) calls only clearSession(). notificationStore is in-memory zustand (no persist), so user A's notifications/badges survive logout until a refetch.

issue: logout() only calls clearSession() + TruecallerAuth.clear(); it never resets the in-memory activeFarmStore. selectedFarm/selectedPond/activeCycle/currentDOC (farm name, pond, species, seed count, feed price) survive logout. On a shared device, when user B logs in without an app restart, HomeScreen.tsx:37 reuses the still-truthy selectedFarm?.id (it only auto-picks a farm when unset) and calls getDashboardSummary with user A's farmId — user A's farm data is shown to user B.
fix: In logout() (and clearSession()) call useActiveFarmStore.getState().clearAll() to drop the previous user's farm/pond/cycle context.
note: logout()->clearSession() never clears in-memory activeFarmStore (clearAll exists at :78 but unused); HomeScreen:37 reuses stale selectedFarm.id, and backend getDashboardSummary (reports.service.ts:33) has NO farm-ownership check, so user B actually receives user A's farm data. Real cross-user leak, compounded by backend IDOR.

area: Caching & staleness (in-memory store not cleared on logout)
issue: uploadStore has no reset and is never cleared on logout; user A's pending/failed photo-upload jobs (localUri + entityLocalId) carry into user B's session and can be retried/uploaded under user B's token, mis-attributing user A's local images.
fix: Add a reset action to uploadStore and call it from authStore.logout().
note: uploadStore is in-memory (no persist), has no reset action, and is not cleared by authStore.logout(); pending jobs (localUri/entityLocalId) survive into next user's session. LOW correct.

area: Dark mode — dead code / false support
issue: A full `dark` color-role palette is exported but never referenced anywhere; all 118 screens/components hardcode `theme.roles.light.*`, StatusBar is hardcoded `barStyle="dark-content"`, and no `useColorScheme`/`Appearance` hook exists. index.ts:13's comment admits dark mode is not wired. The dark palette is unreachable dead flexibility.
fix: Either delete the unused `dark` export or introduce a `useTheme()` hook driven by `useColorScheme()` and replace `roles.light` references with it.
note: dark palette is dead for rendering: no useColorScheme/Appearance, StatusBar hardcoded dark-content, index.ts TODO admits not wired. Minor factual nit: it IS referenced in theme/index.ts (import + roles.dark), so 'never referenced anywhere' is inaccurate; it's unused dead flexibility, a cleanup not a defect.

issue: The single crash-report choke-point is console-only for a pre-launch consumer app; ErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch and every reportError() call just console.error, so no Sentry/Crashlytics DSN is wired and every crash on a real user device is invisible to the team.
fix: Wire `Sentry.captureException(error, { extra: context })` here behind an EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN and add @sentry/react-native init before launch.
note: reportError.ts:9-11 is console.error only; ErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch routes through reportError; no Sentry/Crashlytics DSN wired. Single choke-point confirmed. HIGH defensible for pre-launch consumer app.

area: Ops — no backend CI gate before auto-deploy
issue: `autoDeploy: true` ships every master commit straight to production, but no GitHub workflow runs backend tests, lint, or typecheck (the four workflows only build the APK and check doc links), so untested/broken backend code auto-deploys to prod.
fix: Add a CI workflow that runs `cd backend && npm ci && npm run build && npm test` on push to master and require it before deploy.
note: render.yaml:11 autoDeploy:true; backend has jest unit + e2e specs but the 4 workflows only build APKs and check doc links — none run backend build/test/lint before deploy.

area: Ops — free plan cold starts undocumented/unmitigated
issue: Service runs on Render `plan: free`, which spins the instance down after ~15 min idle so the first mobile request eats a ~50s cold-start boot; there is no keep-alive cron in render.yaml and OPERATIONS.md never mentions cold starts or a warm- up ping despite the app.module.ts DB comments acknowledging spin-down.
fix: Add an external uptime ping (UptimeRobot/cron-job.org) hitting /api/health/liveness every ~10 min and document the cold-start behavior + mitigation in OPERATIONS §1.
note: render.yaml:6 plan:free with no cron/keep-alive; grep of docs/OPERATIONS.md returns zero cold-start/spin-down/warm-up mention. Confirmed unmitigated + undocumented.

issue: The APK build workflow runs `npm install -g expo-cli` (the legacy global CLI, deprecated and dropped for Expo SDK 46+) before `npx expo prebuild` on an SDK 54 project; the deprecated global CLI is unnecessary and its install/postinstall can fail the job.
fix: Delete the `npm install -g expo-cli` step; `npx expo prebuild` already uses the local SDK-54 CLI.
note: Redundant `npm install -g expo-cli` (legacy deprecated CLI) present at line 23 on an Expo SDK ~54.0.32 project; `npx expo prebuild` uses the local CLI. Real but low-impact CI hygiene/risk; location was 23 not 20.

issue: A Kiro IDE working-spec tree (11 files under .kiro/specs, including .config.kiro dotfiles and qa/gate-*.md) is committed to the repo; these are per-tool authoring artifacts, not shared docs, and duplicate the truecaller material already in docs/reference/.
fix: Add `.kiro/` to .gitignore and git rm the tree, or fold any still-relevant content into docs/.
note: 11 .kiro/specs files are git-tracked (design.md:1 exists); .config.kiro dotfiles + qa/gate-*.md are per-tool authoring artifacts, .kiro not in .gitignore. IMPROVEMENT stands.

issue: Captured run-output and one-off debug scripts are committed to the repo: backend/output.txt, backend/output_regression.txt (identical 2FA test logs), frontend/web_output.log (a Metro boot log), backend/test-email.js, backend/verify_2fa.mjs, backend/verify_link_endpoint.mjs — all localhost throwaway artifacts; .gitignore ignores auth-smoke.sh but leaves these tracked.
fix: git rm these files and add `*output*.txt`, `web_output.log`, `verify_*.mjs`, `test-email.js` to .gitignore.
note: backend/output.txt, output_regression.txt (differ only in a random TOTP secret), frontend/web_output.log, backend/test-email.js, verify_2fa.mjs, verify_link_endpoint.mjs all git-tracked; .gitignore ignores auth-smoke.sh but not these.

issue: backend/scripts/verify_2fa.ts and backend/verify_2fa.mjs are the same manual 2FA smoke script duplicated in two locations, both pointing at hardcoded http://localhost:8080; only one (or neither) is needed.
fix: Delete backend/verify_2fa.mjs and keep the scripts/ copy (or delete both — auth-smoke.sh already covers this).
note: backend/scripts/verify_2fa.ts and backend/verify_2fa.mjs are the same 2FA smoke script (identical lines, both hardcode http://localhost:8080); both tracked. Duplicate confirmed.

issue: App offers 6 languages (Hindi/Tamil/Telugu/Bengali/Odia) and localizes all UI strings, but the engine/Morning-Briefing alert titles, bodies and step lists ('Toxic ammonia', 'Stop or sharply reduce feeding', etc.) are hardcoded English in the backend and never pass through i18n, so a Tamil/Hindi user gets English alerts on a headline feature.
fix: Return stable i18n keys + interpolation data from the alert engine and translate client-side, or localize per the user's language preference server-side.

issue: The `computedDOC` entity getter duplicates the same UTC-vs-IST DOC bug as crops.service.computeDOC (new Date(stockingDate) at UTC midnight vs now, floored), so any response serialized from the entity undercounts DOC by 1 during 00:00–05:30 IST — inconsistent with, and buggy alongside, the service path.
fix: Delete the entity getter and use the (IST-corrected) service computeDOC as the single source, or apply the same toIstDateString-based calendar diff here.
note: crop.entity.ts computedDOC (used at pond-context.service.ts:382) floors UTC-instant minus UTC-midnight date; undercounts DOC by 1 during 00:00-05:30 IST, ignoring toIstDateString. Bug real; but finding's claim that service computeDOC is 'IST-corrected' is false — crops.service.computeDOC has the identical bug.

issue: Crop/entity DOC uses stocking-day = 0 (`diffDays + initialAgeDays`), but measurement.service.deriveDoc (backend/src/measurement/measurement.service.ts:248) uses stocking-day = 1 (`diffDays + 1`). The same pond therefore shows DOC N on the crop card and DOC N+1 in measurement history for the same day, a user-facing inconsistency.
fix: Pick one convention (stocking day = 1 is standard for DOC) and apply it in both crops and measurement DOC computations.
note: crop/entity DOC = diffDays + initialAgeDays (stocking day 0); measurement.service.deriveDoc = diffDays + 1 (stocking day 1) and ignores initialAgeDays. Same pond/day shows differing DOC across surfaces. Confirmed user-facing inconsistency.

issue: computeDOC parses the DATE column `stockingDate` ('YYYY-MM-DD') as UTC midnight and diffs it against `now` (absolute instant), then floors by 24h. For IST farmers this returns the UTC calendar-day count, so between 00:00–05:30 IST it undercounts DOC by 1 — exactly during the dawn pond check that drives feed tables and dosing. The repo already has `toIstDateString` for this but DOC was never migrated.
fix: Compute DOC from IST calendar dates: `Math.floor((Date.parse(toIstDateString(now)) - Date.parse(String(crop.stockingDate))) / 86400000) + (crop.initialAgeDays||0)`.
note: crops.service.ts:149-153 parses DATE-typed stockingDate ('YYYY-MM-DD') as UTC midnight (=05:30 IST) and floors (now-stocked)/86400000; DOC ticks at 05:30 IST so 00:00-05:30 IST undercounts by 1. toIstDateString exists in common/ist-date.ts but DOC was never migrated. Same bug duplicated in crop.entity.ts:150-156.

issue: name is @IsString() only — an empty string '' (and unbounded 1000-char / emoji / RTL text) is accepted as a crop name, producing blank-named cycles in lists and reports.
fix: Add @IsNotEmpty() and @MaxLength(120) to name.
note: create-crop.dto.ts:16-17 name is @IsString() only; '' and unbounded/emoji/RTL accepted. LOW correct.

issue: stockingCount is @IsInt() @IsOptional() with NO @Min(0) (unlike totalSeed which has @Min(0)); a negative stockingCount is accepted and becomes the denominator for survival rate, stocking density and biomass back-estimates, yielding negative/garbage KPIs.
fix: Add @Min(1) to stockingCount (and @Min(0) to stockingDensity on line 33).
note: line 35-37 stockingCount @IsInt @IsOptional, no @Min; totalSeed (55) does have @Min(0). Negative value flows to stockingDensity=count/area (crops.service:39) and biomass=count*sr/100 (shrimp-calculations:85), yielding negative KPIs. pond-context:161 clamps its own use to >=0 but density/biomass paths do not.

issue: targetSrPercent is @Min(0) with no @Max(100); a survival-rate target of 150% is accepted and propagates into expected-harvest/biomass projections as >100% survival.
fix: Add @Max(100) to targetSrPercent.
note: line 78-81 targetSrPercent @Min(0) with no @Max(100); 150% accepted. LOW correct.

area: Content & copy — date formatting for Indian users
issue: Password-changed security email interpolates `new Date().toLocaleString()` with no locale or timezone, so it renders the server's default (US 'M/D/YYYY, h:MM AM' in UTC) to Indian users instead of DD/MM/YYYY IST.
fix: Use `new Date().toLocaleString('en-IN', { timeZone: 'Asia/Kolkata' })`.
note: email.service.ts:222 new Date().toLocaleString() with no locale/timeZone args; renders server default (UTC on Render). Cosmetic-on-security-email; MEDIUM slightly high but defect is real.

issue: getDailyFeedUsage builds the day window with `setHours(0,0,0,0)`/`setHours(23,59,59,999)`, which uses the server's local timezone. On Render the backend runs in UTC (no TZ env set), so the 'today' window is 05:30 IST→05:30 IST. Feed logged 00:00–05:30 IST (early-morning feeding) lands in yesterday, so the HomeScreen 'todayFeedUsage' stat (reports.service.ts:56) undercounts on dawn visits.
fix: Bucket by IST: derive the day from `toIstDateString(date)` and query `recordedAt BETWEEN dayStartUTC AND dayEndUTC` where dayStartUTC = that IST date minus the 5:30 offset.
note: getDailyFeedUsage (feed-records.service.ts:168-187) builds window with setHours(0/23...) in server-local tz; called with new Date() from reports.service.ts:56. On UTC server the 'today' window is 05:30 IST->05:30 IST, so 00:00-05:30 IST feed undercounts todayFeedUsage.

issue: harvestDate is only @IsDateString with no upper bound or ordering against the crop's stockingDate; a future harvestDate is accepted and, for harvestType='full', written as actualHarvestDate by closeCycle → negative/absurd cycle length and DOC-based metrics.
fix: Reject harvestDate in the future and before the crop stockingDate in HarvestsService.create (compare against crop.stockingDate / now).
note: create-harvest.dto.ts:14 harvestDate @IsDateString only; HarvestsService.create (harvests.service.ts:19-31) does no future/ordering guard and passes harvestDate straight to closeCycle for harvestType='full'. Future/pre-stocking date accepted.

issue: weightKg (and count, averageSize, salePriceTotal) are @IsNumber() with no @Min(0); POST /harvests with weightKg:-500 or 0 is persisted and, for harvestType='full', flows into closeCycle → corrupts FCR (feed/harvest), survival rate, biomass and P&L (negative/absurd yield). UpdateHarvestDto.weightKg is likewise unbounded.
fix: Add @Min(0) to weightKg/count/averageSize/salePriceTotal in both create and update DTOs (weightKg @Min(0.01)).
note: create-harvest.dto.ts weightKg/count/averageSize/salePriceTotal are @IsNumber with no @Min (update DTO same). Corruption path is via persisted weightKg feeding calculateFcr()/runningFcr()/biomass — NOT via closeCycle(), which only sets status=completed and unlinks the pond and never reads weightKg. Mechanism in the finding is inaccurate but the missing-bound defect and downstream FCR/analytics corruption are real. HIGH stands.

issue: deriveDoc returns Math.floor((measuredAt - stocking)/day)+1 with no floor at 1; a measurement dated before the crop stockingDate (or a future stockingDate) yields a zero/negative DOC that mislabels every downstream trend/engine bucket.
fix: Return diffDays < 0 ? null : diffDays + 1 (or clamp to 1).
note: measurement.service.ts:248 returns diffDays+1 with no clamp; measuredAt before stockingDate yields zero/negative DOC. No caller guard.

area: backend DTO / sampling date & text bounds
issue: samplingDate is only @IsDateString (future dates accepted) and mbwG/notes have no @Max/@MaxLength, so a mid-cycle sampling can be dated in the future and notes can carry unbounded 1000-char/emoji text into reports.
fix: Reject future samplingDate in SamplingService.create and add @MaxLength to notes / a sane @Max to mbwG.
note: samplingDate is @IsDateString only, no future-date reject in SamplingService.create; notes has no @MaxLength; mbwG has @Min(0) but no @Max. All three sub-claims hold.

issue: CalculateSurvivalRateDto has no @Min and no cross-check that harvestedCount <= initialStock; POST /shrimp-calculations/survival-rate {initialStock:100, harvestedCount:250} returns survivalRatePercent:250, and negative inputs return negative SR.
fix: Add @Min(0) to both fields and clamp survivalRate to <=100 (or reject harvestedCount > initialStock) in calculateSurvivalRate.
note: CalculateSurvivalRateDto fields are @IsNumber with no @Min; calculateSurvivalRate (service:59) does (harvested/initial)*100 with no clamp — 100/250 returns 250, negatives return negative.

issue: amount is @IsNumber() with no @Min, while type is constrained to income/expense (sign carried by type). A negative or 0 amount (e.g. type:'income', amount:-5000) is accepted and corrupts P&L totals. Inconsistent with CreateExpenseDto which correctly has @Min(0.01).
fix: Add @Min(0.01) to amount.
note: create-transaction.dto.ts amount is @IsNumber no @Min; sign carried by type income/expense. finances/dto/create-expense.dto.ts:27 has @Min(0.01), confirming the inconsistency. Negative/zero amount corrupts P&L totals.

issue: CONTRIBUTING promises 'Green CI/local gates' and the security/test checklist, but the only CI workflows are docs link-check and on-demand APK builds — no workflow runs backend jest, frontend jest, or tsc, so test/typecheck regressions merge with nothing enforcing them.
fix: Add a PR workflow that runs `cd backend && npm ci && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test` and `cd frontend && npm ci && npx tsc --noEmit && npx jest`.

issue: feedApi.getAll returns the backend PageDto envelope {data, meta} (feed-records.service.ts:131), not an array. `[...r.data].sort(...)` spreads a plain non-iterable object and throws TypeError, which is swallowed by the surrounding `.catch(() => null)`, so the dashboard 'last feed' glance card is permanently blank.
fix: Unwrap first: `[...(r.data.data ?? [])].sort(...)`.
note: GET /feed-records returns PageDto {data,meta} (feed-records.service.ts returns PageDto; controller findAll passes it through). feedApi.getAll mistypes it as FeedRecord[], so r.data is the {data,meta} object; [...r.data] spreads a non-iterable object → TypeError → swallowed by .catch(()=>null). Sibling FeedStatsScreen.tsx:55 explicitly unwraps with toRecords and a comment noting the PageDto shape, proving the bug. Card stays blank.

issue: Client validation is only `!stockingCount || isNaN(parseInt(stockingCount))`; `parseInt('-500')`=-500 and `parseInt('500abc')`=500 both pass, so negative counts and trailing-garbage numbers reach the API (which also lacks @Min on stockingCount).

note: Client check at :54 is !stockingCount||isNaN(parseInt(...)); parseInt('-500')=-500 and parseInt('500abc')=500 pass. Backend create-crop.dto.ts:35-37 has only @IsInt()/@IsOptional(), no @Min/@IsPositive, so negatives reach the DB. Confirmed.

issue: pondsApi.getAll(farmId) is called with no take param; backend /ponds is paginated with default take:10 (ponds.controller.ts:49 + PageOptionsDto), so a farm with more than 10 ponds silently shows only 10 in the farm detail list.

issue: feedApi.getByCrop/getAll are called once with no take param and the screen has no pagination UI; backend defaults to take:10 (feed-records.service.ts:101), so the feed history list silently shows at most 10 records.

issue: waterQualityApi.getAll(pondId) is called once with no take param and no pagination UI; backend water-quality findAll returns PageDto with default take:10 (water-quality.service.ts:184), so the water-quality history silently caps at 10 records.

area: Android native SDK in shared bundle
issue: authStore (loaded on every platform at startup) imports TruecallerAuth, whose module top-level unconditionally imports the Android-only native SDK `@dhana-cs/react-native-truecaller` (TruecallerAuth.ts:17). This pulls an Android-only native module into the iOS and web bundle graphs even though every call site guards on isAndroid; runtime is safe on iOS (clear() is a pure no-op) but the module and its side-effects still bundle/evaluate off-Android.
fix: Lazy-require trueCallerService inside the isUsable/initialize/authenticate methods (behind the isAndroid guard) so non-Android bundles never load the native SDK.
note: TruecallerAuth.ts:17 has unconditional top-level import of @dhana-cs/react-native-truecaller; authStore.ts:7 imports it eagerly, so native SDK enters iOS/web bundle graphs. Runtime guarded by isAndroid. IMPROVEMENT correct.

issue: scheduleDailyWaterQualityReminders/scheduleWeeklyChemistryReminder (the 'continuous-data loop' reminders the header comment calls core, 'works offline, no server needed') call Notifications.scheduleNotificationAsync, which is unsupported on web and throws — caught and only console.warn'd. Combined with registerForPushNotificationsAsync returning undefined on web (Device.isDevice false), web users get zero reminders and zero push, silently.
fix: Early-return on Platform.OS==='web' with a user-visible note, or drop web from supported platforms so the feature gap is explicit.
note: scheduleNotificationAsync calls (l.90,135) wrapped in try/catch→console.warn only; registerForPushNotificationsAsync returns undefined when !Device.isDevice (web). Silent web reminder gap confirmed; LOW correct.
